116/**
117 * Utility class for interfacing with remote REST interfaces.
118 *
119 * <p>
120 * Built upon the feature-rich Apache HttpClient library, the Juneau RestClient API adds support for fluent-style
121 * REST calls and the ability to perform marshalling of POJOs to and from HTTP parts.
122 *
123 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
124 * <p class='bjava'>
125 *    <jc>// Create a basic REST client with JSON support and download a bean.</jc>
126 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>()
127 *       .json5()
128 *       .build()
129 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
130 *       .run()
131 *       .assertStatus().asCode().is(200)
132 *       .assertHeader(<js>"Content-Type"</js>).matchesSimple(<js>"application/json*"</js>)
133 *       .getContent().as(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
134 * </p>
135 *
136 * <p>
137 * Breaking apart the fluent call, we can see the classes being used:
138 * <p class='bjava'>
139 *    RestClient.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json5();
140 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jv>builder</jv>.build();
141 *    RestRequest <jv>req</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>);
142 *    RestResponse <jv>res</jv> = <jv>req</jv>.run();
143 *    RestResponseStatusLineAssertion <jv>statusLineAssertion</jv> = <jv>res</jv>.assertStatus();
144 *    FluentIntegerAssertion&lt;RestResponse&gt; <jv>codeAssertion</jv> = <jv>statusLineAssertion</jv>.asCode();
145 *    <jv>res</jv> = <jv>codeAssertion</jv>.is(200);
146 *    FluentStringAssertion&lt;RestResponse&gt; <jv>headerAssertion</jv> = <jv>res</jv>.assertHeader(<js>"Content-Type"</js>);
147 *    <jv>res</jv> = <jv>headerAssertion</jv>.matchesSimple(<js>"application/json*"</js>);
148 *    RestResponseBody <jv>content</jv> = <jv>res</jv>.getContent();
149 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>content</jv>.as(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
150 * </p>
151 *
152 * <p>
153 * It additionally provides support for creating remote proxy interfaces using REST as the transport medium.
154 *
155 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
156 * <p class='bjava'>
157 *    <jc>// Define a Remote proxy for interacting with a REST interface.</jc>
158 *    <ja>@Remote</ja>(path=<js>"/petstore"</js>)
159 *    <jk>public interface</jk> PetStore {
160 *
161 *       <ja>@RemotePost</ja>(<js>"/pets"</js>)
162 *       Pet addPet(
163 *          <ja>@Content</ja> CreatePet <jv>pet</jv>,
164 *          <ja>@Header</ja>(<js>"E-Tag"</js>) UUID <jv>etag</jv>,
165 *          <ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"debug"</js>) <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>debug</jv>
166 *       );
167 *    }
168 *
169 *    <jc>// Use a RestClient with default JSON 5 support.</jc>
170 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json5().build();
171 *
172 *    PetStore <jv>store</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.getRemote(PetStore.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"http://localhost:10000"</js>);
173 *    CreatePet <jv>createPet</jv> = <jk>new</jk> CreatePet(<js>"Fluffy"</js>, 9.99);
174 *    Pet <jv>pet</jv> = <jv>store</jv>.addPet(<jv>createPet</jv>, UUID.<jsm>randomUUID</jsm>(), <jk>true</jk>);
175 * </p>
176 *
177 * <p>
178 * The classes are closely tied to Apache HttpClient, yet provide lots of additional functionality:
179 * <ul class='javatree'>
180 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestClient} <jk>extends</jk> {@link HttpClient}, creates {@link RestRequest} objects.
181 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest} <jk>extends</jk> {@link HttpUriRequest}, creates {@link RestResponse} objects.
182 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestResponse} creates {@link ResponseContent} and {@link ResponseHeader} objects.
183 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ResponseContent} <jk>extends</jk> {@link HttpEntity}
184 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ResponseHeader} <jk>extends</jk> {@link Header}
185 * </ul>
186 *
187 *
188 * <p>
189 * Instances of this class are built using the {@link Builder} class which can be constructed using
190 * the {@link #create() RestClient.create()} method as shown above.
191 *
192 * <p>
193 * Clients are typically created with a root URI so that relative URIs can be used when making requests.
194 * This is done using the {@link Builder#rootUrl(Object)} method.
195 *
196 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
197 * <p class='bjava'>
198 *    <jc>// Create a client where all URIs are relative to localhost.</jc>
199 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json().rootUrl(<js>"http://localhost:5000"</js>).build();
200 *
201 *    <jc>// Use relative paths.</jc>
202 *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<js>"/subpath"</js>).run().getContent().asString();
203 * </p>
204 *
205 * <p>
206 * The {@link RestClient} class creates {@link RestRequest} objects using the following methods:
207 *
208 * <ul class='javatree'>
209 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestClient}
210 *    <ul>
211 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#get(Object) get(uri)} / {@link RestClient#get() get()}
212 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#put(Object,Object) put(uri,body)} / {@link RestClient#put(Object) put(uri)}
213 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#post(Object) post(uri,body)} / {@link RestClient#post(Object) post(uri)}
214 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#patch(Object,Object) patch(uri,body)} / {@link RestClient#patch(Object) patch(uri)}
215 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#delete(Object) delete(uri)}
216 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#head(Object) head(uri)}
217 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#options(Object) options(uri)}
218 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#formPost(Object,Object) formPost(uri,body)} / {@link RestClient#formPost(Object) formPost(uri)}
219 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#formPostPairs(Object,String...) formPostPairs(uri,parameters...)}
220 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestClient#request(String,Object,Object) request(method,uri,body)}
221 *    </ul>
222 * </ul>
223 *
224 * <p>
225 * The {@link RestRequest} class creates {@link RestResponse} objects using the following methods:
226 *
227 * <ul class='javatree'>
228 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
229 *    <ul>
230 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#run() run()}
231 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#complete() complete()}
232 *    </ul>
233 * </ul>
234 *
235 * <p>
236 * The distinction between the two methods is that {@link RestRequest#complete() complete()} automatically consumes the response body and
237 * {@link RestRequest#run() run()} does not.  Note that you must consume response bodies in order for HTTP connections to be freed up
238 * for reuse!  The {@link InputStream InputStreams} returned by the {@link ResponseContent} object are auto-closing once
239 * they are exhausted, so it is often not necessary to explicitly close them.
240 *
241 * <p>
242 * The following examples show the distinction between the two calls:
243 *
244 * <p class='bjava'>
245 *    <jc>// Consuming the response, so use run().</jc>
246 *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).run().getContent().asString();
247 *
248 *    <jc>// Only interested in response status code, so use complete().</jc>
249 *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>status</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).complete().getStatusCode();
250 * </p>
251 *
252 *
253 * <h4 class='topic'>POJO Marshalling</h4>
254 *
255 * <p>
256 * By default, JSON support is provided for HTTP request and response bodies.
257 * Other languages can be specified using any of the following builder methods:
258 * <ul class='javatree'>
259 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
260 *    <ul>
261 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#json() json()}
262 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#json5() json5()}
263 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#xml() xml()}
264 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#html() html()}
265 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#plainText() plainText()}
266 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#bson() bson()}
267 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#msgPack() msgPack()}
268 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#uon() uon()}
269 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#urlEnc() urlEnc()}
270 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#openApi() openApi()}
271 *    </ul>
272 * </ul>
273 *
274 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
275 * <p class='bjava'>
276 *    <jc>// Create a basic REST client with JSON 5 support.</jc>
277 *    <jc>// Typically easier to use when performing unit tests.</jc>
278 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json5().build();
279 * </p>
280 *
281 * <p>
282 * Clients can also support multiple languages:
283 *
284 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
285 * <p class='bjava'>
286 *    <jc>// Create a REST client with support for multiple languages.</jc>
287 *    RestClient <jv>client1</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json().xml().openApi().build();
288 *
289 *    <jc>// Create a REST client with support for all supported languages.</jc>
290 *    RestClient <jv>client2</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().universal().build();
291 * </p>
292 *
293 * <p>
294 * When using clients with multiple language support, you must specify the <c>Content-Type</c> header on requests
295 * with bodies to specify which serializer should be selected.
296 *
297 * <p class='bjava'>
298 *    <jc>// Create a REST client with support for multiple languages.</jc>
299 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().universal().build();
300 *
301 *    <jv>client</jv>
302 *       .post(<jsf>URI</jsf>, <jv>myBean</jv>)
303 *       .contentType(<js>"application/json"</js>)
304 *       .complete()
305 *       .assertStatus().is(200);
306 * </p>
307 *
308 * <p>
309 * Languages can also be specified per-request.
310 *
311 * <p class='bjava'>
312 *    <jc>// Create a REST client with no default languages supported.</jc>
313 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().build();
314 *
315 *    <jc>// Use JSON for this request.</jc>
316 *    <jv>client</jv>
317 *       .post(<jsf>URI</jsf>, <jv>myBean</jv>)
318 *       .json()
319 *       .complete()
320 *       .assertStatus().is(200);
321 * </p>
322 *
323 *
324 * <p>
325 * The {@link Builder} class provides convenience methods for setting common serializer and parser
326 * settings.
327 *
328 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
329 * <p class='bjava'>
330 *    <jc>// Create a basic REST client with JSON support.</jc>
331 *    <jc>// Use single-quotes and whitespace.</jc>
332 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json().sq().ws().build();
333 * </p>
334 *
335 * <p>
336 *    Other methods are also provided for specifying the serializers and parsers used for lower-level marshalling support:
337 * <ul class='javatree'>
338 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
339 *    <ul>
340 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#serializer(Serializer) serializer(Serializer)}
341 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#parser(Parser) parser(Parser)}
342 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#marshaller(Marshaller) marshaller(Marshaller)}
343 *    </ul>
344 * </ul>
345 *
346 * <p>
347 * HTTP parts (headers, query parameters, form data...) are serialized and parsed using the {@link HttpPartSerializer}
348 * and {@link HttpPartParser} APIs.  By default, clients are configured to use {@link OpenApiSerializer} and
349 * {@link OpenApiParser}.  These can be overridden using the following methods:
350 * <ul class='javatree'>
351 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
352 *    <ul>
353 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#partSerializer(Class) partSerializer(Class&lt;? extends HttpPartSerializer>)}
354 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#partParser(Class) partParser(Class&lt;? extends HttpPartParser>)}
355 *    </ul>
356 * </ul>
357 *
358 *
359 * <h4 class='topic'>Request Headers</h4>
360 * <p>
361 * Per-client or per-request headers can be specified using the following methods:
362 * <ul class='javatree'>
363 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
364 *    <ul>
365 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#headers() headerData()}
366 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#header(String,String) header(String,Object)}
367 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#header(String,Supplier) header(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
368 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#headers(Header...) headers(Header...)}
369 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#headersDefault(Header...) defaultHeaders(Header...)}
370 *    </ul>
371 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
372 *    <ul>
373 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#header(String,Object) header(String,Object)}
374 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#headers(Header...) headers(Header...)}
375 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#headersBean(Object) headersBean(Object)}
376 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#headerPairs(String...) headerPairs(String...)}
377 *    </ul>
378 * </ul>
379 *
380 * <p>
381 * The supplier methods are particularly useful for header values whose values may change over time (such as <c>Authorization</c> headers
382 * which may need to change every few minutes).
383 * </p>
384 *
385 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
386 * <p class='bjava'>
387 *    <jc>// Create a client that adds a dynamic Authorization header to every request.</jc>
388 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().header(<js>"Authorization"</js>, ()-&gt;getMyAuthToken()).build();
389 * </p>
390 *
391 * <p>
392 * The {@link HttpPartSchema} API allows you to define OpenAPI schemas to POJO data structures on both requests
393 * and responses.
394 *
395 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
396 * <p class='bjava'>
397 *    <jc>// Create a client that adds a header "Foo: bar|baz" to every request.</jc>
398 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>()
399 *       .header(<js>"Foo"</js>, AList.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"bar"</js>,<js>"baz"</js>), <jsf>T_ARRAY_PIPES</jsf>)
400 *       .build();
401 * </p>
402 *
403 * <p>
404 * The methods with {@link ListOperation} parameters allow you to control whether new headers get appended, prepended, or
405 * replace existing headers with the same name.
406 *
407 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
408 *    <li class='note'>Methods that pass in POJOs convert values to strings using the part serializers.  Methods that pass in <c>Header</c> or
409 *       <c>NameValuePair</c> objects use the values returned by that bean directly.
410 * </ul>
411 *
412 *
413 * <h4 class='topic'>Request Query Parameters</h4>
414 * <p>
415 * Per-client or per-request query parameters can be specified using the following methods:
416 * <ul class='javatree'>
417 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
418 *    <ul>
419 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#queryData() queryData()}
420 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#queryData(String,String) queryData(String,String)}
421 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#queryData(String,Supplier) queryData(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
422 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#queryData(NameValuePair...) queryData(NameValuePair...)}
423 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#queryDataDefault(NameValuePair...) defaultQueryData(NameValuePair...)}
424 *    </ul>
425 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
426 *    <ul>
427 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#queryData(String,Object) queryData(String,Object)}
428 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#queryData(NameValuePair...) queryData(NameValuePair...)}
429 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#queryDataBean(Object) queryDataBean(Object)}
430 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#queryCustom(Object) queryCustom(Object)}
431 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#queryDataPairs(String...) queryDataPairs(String...)}
432 *    </ul>
433 * </ul>
434 *
435 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
436 * <p class='bjava'>
437 *    <jc>// Create a client that adds a ?foo=bar query parameter to every request.</jc>
438 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().query(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>).build();
439 *
440 *    <jc>// Or do it on every request.</jc>
441 *    String <jv>response</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).query(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>).run().getContent().asString();
442 * </p>
443 *
444 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
445 *    <li class='note'>Like header values, dynamic values and OpenAPI schemas are supported.
446 *    <li class='note'>Methods that pass in POJOs convert values to strings using the part serializers.  Methods that pass in <c>NameValuePair</c>
447 *       objects use the values returned by that bean directly.
448 * </ul>
449 *
450 *
451 * <h4 class='topic'>Request Form Data</h4>
452 *
453 * <p>
454 * Per-client or per-request form-data parameters can be specified using the following methods:
455 * <ul class='javatree'>
456 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
457 *    <ul>
458 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#formData() formData()}
459 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#formData(String,String) formData(String,String)}
460 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#formData(String,Supplier) formData(String,Supplier&lt;?&gt;)}
461 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#formData(NameValuePair...) formDatas(NameValuePair...)}
462 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#formDataDefault(NameValuePair...) defaultFormData(NameValuePair...)}
463 *    </ul>
464 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
465 *    <ul>
466 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#formData(String,Object) formData(String,Object)}
467 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#formData(NameValuePair...) formData(NameValuePair...)}
468 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#formDataBean(Object) formDataBean(Object)}
469 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#formDataCustom(Object) formDataCustom(Object)}
470 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#formDataPairs(String...) formDataPairs(String...)}
471 *    </ul>
472 * </ul>
473 *
474 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
475 *    <li class='note'>Like header values, dynamic values and OpenAPI schemas are supported.
476 *    <li class='note'>Methods that pass in POJOs convert values to strings using the part serializers.  Methods that pass in <c>NameValuePair</c>
477 *       objects use the values returned by that bean directly.
478 * </ul>
479 *
480 *
481 * <h4 class='topic'>Request Body</h4>
482 *
483 * <p>
484 * The request body can either be passed in with the client creator method (e.g. {@link RestClient#post(Object,Object) post(uri,body)}),
485 * or can be specified via the following methods:
486 *
487 * <ul class='javatree'>
488 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
489 *    <ul>
490 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#content(Object) body(Object)}
491 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#content(Object,HttpPartSchema) body(Object,HttpPartSchema)}
492 *    </ul>
493 * </ul>
494 *
495 * <p>
496 * The request body can be any of the following types:
497 * <ul class='javatree'>
498 *       <li class='jc'>
499 *          {@link Object} - POJO to be converted to text using the {@link Serializer} defined on the client or request.
500 *       <li class='jc'>
501 *          {@link Reader} - Raw contents of {@code Reader} will be serialized to remote resource.
502 *       <li class='jc'>
503 *          {@link InputStream} - Raw contents of {@code InputStream} will be serialized to remote resource.
504 *       <li class='jc'>
505 *          {@link HttpResource} - Raw contents will be serialized to remote resource.  Additional headers and media type will be set on request.
506 *       <li class='jc'>
507 *          {@link HttpEntity} - Bypass Juneau serialization and pass HttpEntity directly to HttpClient.
508 *       <li class='jc'>
509 *          {@link PartList} - Converted to a URL-encoded FORM post.
510 *       <li class='jc'>
511 *          {@link Supplier} - A supplier of anything on this list.
512 *    </ul>
513 *
514 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
515 *    <li class='note'>If the serializer on the client or request is explicitly set to <jk>null</jk>, POJOs will be converted to strings
516 *       using the registered part serializer as content type <js>"text/plain</js>.  If the part serializer is also <jk>null</jk>,
517 *       POJOs will be converted to strings using {@link ClassMeta#toString(Object)} which typically just calls {@link Object#toString()}.
518 * </ul>
519 *
520 *
521 * <h4 class='topic'>Response Status</h4>
522 *
523 * <p>
524 * After execution using {@link RestRequest#run()} or {@link RestRequest#complete()}, the following methods can be used
525 * to get the response status:
526 *
527 * <ul class='javatree'>
528 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestResponse}
529 *    <ul>
530 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getStatusLine() getStatusLine()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link StatusLine}</c>
531 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getStatusCode() getStatusCode()} <jk>returns</jk> <jk>int</jk></c>
532 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getReasonPhrase() getReasonPhrase()} <jk>returns</jk> String</c>
533 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#assertStatus() assertStatus()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link FluentResponseStatusLineAssertion}</c>
534 *    </ul>
535 * </ul>
536 *
537 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
538 * <p class='bjava'>
539 *    <jc>// Only interested in status code.</jc>
540 *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>statusCode</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).complete().getStatusCode();
541 * </p>
542 *
543 * <p>
544 * Equivalent methods with mutable parameters are provided to allow access to status values without breaking fluent call chains.
545 *
546 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
547 * <p class='bjava'>
548 *    <jc>// Interested in multiple values.</jc>
549 *    Holder&lt;Integer&gt; <jv>statusCode</jv> = Holder.<jsm>create</jsm>();
550 *    Holder&lt;String&gt; <jv>reasonPhrase</jv> = Holder.<jsm>create</jsm>();
551 *
552 *    <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).complete().getStatusCode(<jv>statusCode</jv>).getReasonPhrase(<jv>reasonPhrase</jv>);
553 *    System.<jsf>err</jsf>.println(<js>"statusCode="</js>+<jv>statusCode</jv>.get()+<js>", reasonPhrase="</js>+<jv>reasonPhrase</jv>.get());
554 * </p>
555 *
556 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
557 *    <li class='note'>If you are only interested in the response status and not the response body, be sure to use {@link RestRequest#complete()} instead
558 *       of {@link RestRequest#run()} to make sure the response body gets automatically cleaned up.  Otherwise you must
559 *       consume the response yourself.
560 * </ul>
561 *
562 * <p>
563 * The assertion method is provided for quickly asserting status codes in fluent calls.
564 *
565 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
566 * <p class='bjava'>
567 *    <jc>// Status assertion using a static value.</jc>
568 *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
569 *       .run()
570 *       .assertStatus().asCode().isBetween(200,399)
571 *       .getContent().asString();
572 *
573 *    <jc>// Status assertion using a predicate.</jc>
574 *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
575 *       .run()
576 *       .assertStatus().asCode().is(<jv>x</jv> -&gt; <jv>x</jv>&lt;400)
577 *       .getContent().asString();
578 * </p>
579 *
580 *
581 * <h4 class='topic'>Response Headers</h4>
582 *
583 * <p>
584 * Response headers are accessed through the following methods:
585 *
586 * <ul class='javatree'>
587 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestResponse}
588 *    <ul>
589 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getHeaders(String) getHeaders(String)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link ResponseHeader}[]</c>
590 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getFirstHeader(String) getFirstHeader(String)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link ResponseHeader}</c>
591 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getLastHeader(String) getLastHeader(String)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link ResponseHeader}</c>
592 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getAllHeaders() getAllHeaders()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link ResponseHeader}[]</c>
593 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getStringHeader(String) getStringHeader(String)} <jk>returns</jk> String</c>
594 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#containsHeader(String) containsHeader(String)} <jk>returns</jk> <jk>boolean</jk></c>
595 *    </ul>
596 * </ul>
597 *
598 * <p>
599 * The {@link RestResponse#getFirstHeader(String)} and {@link RestResponse#getLastHeader(String)} methods return an empty {@link ResponseHeader} object instead of<jk>null</jk>.
600 * This allows it to be used more easily in fluent calls.
601 *
602 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
603 * <p class='bjava'>
604 *    <jc>// See if response contains Location header.</jc>
605 *    <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>hasLocationHeader</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).complete().getLastHeader(<js>"Location"</js>).exists();
606 * </p>
607 *
608 * <p>
609 * The {@link ResponseHeader} class extends from the HttpClient {@link Header} class and provides several convenience
610 * methods:
611 *
612 * <ul class='javatree'>
613 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ResponseHeader}
614 *    <ul>
615 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#isPresent() isPresent()} <jk>returns</jk> <jk>boolean</jk></c>
616 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asString() asString()} <jk>returns</jk> String</c>
617 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#as(Type,Type...) as(Type,Type...)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
618 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#as(Class) as(Class&lt;T&gt;)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
619 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asMatcher(Pattern) asMatcher(Pattern)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link Matcher}</c>
620 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asMatcher(String) asMatcher(String)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link Matcher}</c>
621 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asHeader(Class) asHeader(Class&lt;T <jk>extends</jk> BasicHeader&gt; c)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicHeader}</c>
622 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asStringHeader() asStringHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicStringHeader}</c>
623 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asIntegerHeader() asIntegerHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicIntegerHeader}</c>
624 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asLongHeader() asLongHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicLongHeader}</c>
625 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asDateHeader() asDateHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicDateHeader}</c>
626 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asCsvHeader() asCsvHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicCsvHeader}</c>
627 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asEntityTagsHeader() asEntityTagsHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicEntityTagsHeader}</c>
628 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asStringRangesHeader() asStringRangesHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicStringRangesHeader}</c>
629 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#asUriHeader() asUriHeader()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link BasicUriHeader}</c>
630 *    </ul>
631 * </ul>
632 *
633 * <p>
634 * The {@link ResponseHeader#schema(HttpPartSchema)} method allows you to perform parsing of OpenAPI formats for
635 * header parts.
636 *
637 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
638 * <p class='bjava'>
639 *    <jc>// Parse the header "Foo: bar|baz".</jc>
640 *    List&lt;String&gt; <jv>fooHeader</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
641 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
642 *       .complete()
643 *       .getHeader(<js>"Foo"</js>).schema(<jsf>T_ARRAY_PIPES</jsf>).as(List.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
644 * </p>
645 *
646 * <p>
647 * Assertion methods are also provided for fluent-style calls:
648 *
649 * <ul class='javatree'>
650 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ResponseHeader}
651 *    <ul>
652 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseHeader#assertValue() assertValue()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link FluentResponseHeaderAssertion}</c>
653 *    </ul>
654 * </ul>
655 *
656 * <p>
657 * Note how in the following example, the fluent assertion returns control to the {@link RestResponse} object after
658 * the assertion has been completed:
659 *
660 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
661 * <p class='bjava'>
662 *    <jc>// Assert the response content type is any sort of JSON.</jc>
663 *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
664 *       .run()
665 *       .getHeader(<js>"Content-Type"</js>).assertValue().matchesSimple(<js>"application/json*"</js>)
666 *       .getContent().asString();
667 * </p>
668 *
669 *
670 * <h4 class='topic'>Response Body</h4>
671 *
672 * <p>
673 * The response body is accessed through the following method:
674 *
675 * <ul class='javatree'>
676 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestResponse}
677 *    <ul>
678 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestResponse#getContent() getContent()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link ResponseContent}</c>
679 *    </ul>
680 * </ul>
681 *
682 * <p>
683 * The {@link ResponseContent} class extends from the HttpClient {@link HttpEntity} class and provides several convenience
684 * methods:
685 *
686 * <ul class='javatree'>
687 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ResponseContent}
688 *    <ul>
689 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asInputStream() asInputStream()} <jk>returns</jk> InputStream</c>
690 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asReader() asReader()} <jk>returns</jk> Reader</c>
691 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asReader(Charset) asReader(Charset)} <jk>returns</jk> Reader</c>
692 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#pipeTo(OutputStream) pipeTo(OutputStream)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link RestResponse}</c>
693 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#pipeTo(Writer) pipeTo(Writer)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link RestResponse}</c>
694 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#as(Type,Type...) as(Type,Type...)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
695 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#as(Class) as(Class&lt;T&gt;)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
696 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asFuture(Class) asFuture(Class&lt;T&gt;)} <jk>returns</jk> Future&lt;T&gt;</c>
697 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asFuture(Type,Type...) asFuture(Type,Type...)} <jk>returns</jk> Future&lt;T&gt;</c>
698 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asString() asString()} <jk>returns</jk> String</c>
699 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asStringFuture() asStringFuture()} <jk>returns</jk> Future&lt;String&gt;</c>
700 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asAbbreviatedString(int) asAbbreviatedString(int)} <jk>returns</jk> String</c>
701 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asMatcher(Pattern) asMatcher(Pattern)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link Matcher}</c>
702 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#asMatcher(String) asMatcher(String)} <jk>returns</jk> {@link Matcher}</c>
703 *    </ul>
704 * </ul>
705 *
706 * <br>
707 *
708 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
709 * <p class='bjava'>
710 *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of strings.</jc>
711 *    List&lt;String&gt; <jv>list1</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
712 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
713 *       .run()
714 *       .getContent().as(LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
715 *
716 *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of beans.</jc>
717 *    List&lt;MyBean&gt; <jv>list2</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
718 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
719 *       .run()
720 *       .getContent().as(LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
721 *
722 *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of linked-lists of strings.</jc>
723 *    List&lt;List&lt;String&gt;&gt; <jv>list3</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
724 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
725 *       .run()
726 *       .getContent().as(LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
727 *
728 *    <jc>// Parse into a map of string keys/values.</jc>
729 *    Map&lt;String,String&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
730 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
731 *       .run()
732 *       .getContent().as(TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
733 *
734 *    <jc>// Parse into a map containing string keys and values of lists containing beans.</jc>
735 *    Map&lt;String,List&lt;MyBean&gt;&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
736 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
737 *       .run()
738 *       .getContent().as(TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, List.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
739 * </p>
740 *
741 * <p>
742 * The response body can only be consumed once unless it has been cached into memory.  In many cases, the body is
743 * automatically cached when using the assertions methods or methods such as {@link ResponseContent#asString()}.
744 * However, methods that involve reading directly from the input stream cannot be called twice.
745 * In these cases, the {@link RestResponse#cacheContent()} and {@link ResponseContent#cache()} methods are provided
746 * to cache the response body in memory so that you can perform several operations against it.
747 *
748 * <p class='bjava'>
749 *    <jc>// Cache the response body so we can access it twice.</jc>
750 *    InputStream <jv>inputStream</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
751 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
752 *       .run()
753 *       .cacheBody()
754 *       .getContent().pipeTo(<jv>someOtherStream</jv>)
755 *       .getContent().asInputStream();
756 * </p>
757 *
758 * <p>
759 * Assertion methods are also provided for fluent-style calls:
760 *
761 * <ul class='javatree'>
762 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ResponseContent}
763 *    <ul>
764 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link ResponseContent#assertValue() assertValue()} <jk>returns</jk> {@link FluentResponseBodyAssertion}</c>
765 *    </ul>
766 * </ul>
767 *
768 * <br>
769 *
770 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
771 * <p class='bjava'>
772 *    <jc>// Assert that the body contains the string "Success".</jc>
773 *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
774 *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
775 *       .run()
776 *       .getContent().assertString().contains(<js>"Success"</js>)
777 *       .getContent().asString();
778 * </p>
779 *
780 * <p>
781 * Object assertions allow you to parse the response body into a POJO and then perform various tests on that resulting
782 * POJO.
783 *
784 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
785 * <p class='bjava'>
786 *    <jc>// Parse bean into POJO and then validate that it was parsed correctly.</jc>
787 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
788 *       .run()
789 *       .getContent().assertObject(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>).asJson().is(<js>"{foo:'bar'}"</js>)
790 *       .getContent().as(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
791 * </p>
792 *
793 *
794 * <h4 class='topic'>Custom Call Handlers</h4>
795 *
796 * <p>
797 * The {@link RestCallHandler} interface provides the ability to provide custom handling of requests.
798 *
799 * <ul class='javatree'>
800 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
801 *    <ul>
802 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#callHandler() callHandler()}
803 *    </ul>
804 *    <li class='jic'>{@link RestCallHandler}
805 *    <ul>
806 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestCallHandler#run(HttpHost,HttpRequest,HttpContext) run(HttpHost,HttpRequest,HttpContext)} <jk>returns</jk> HttpResponse</c>
807 *    </ul>
808 * </ul>
809 *
810 * <p>
811 * Note that there are other ways of accomplishing this such as extending the {@link RestClient} class and overriding
812 * the {@link #run(HttpHost,HttpRequest,HttpContext)} method
813 * or by defining your own {@link HttpRequestExecutor}.  Using this interface is often simpler though.
814 *
815 *
816 * <h4 class='topic'>Interceptors</h4>
817 *
818 * <p>
819 * The {@link RestCallInterceptor} API provides a quick way of intercepting and manipulating requests and responses beyond
820 * the existing {@link HttpRequestInterceptor} and {@link HttpResponseInterceptor} APIs.
821 *
822 * <ul class='javatree'>
823 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
824 *    <ul>
825 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#interceptors(Object...) interceptors(Object...)}
826 *    </ul>
827 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestRequest}
828 *    <ul>
829 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#interceptors(RestCallInterceptor...) interceptors(RestCallInterceptor...)}
830 *    </ul>
831 *    <li class='jic'>{@link RestCallInterceptor}
832 *    <ul>
833 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestCallInterceptor#onInit(RestRequest) onInit(RestRequest)}
834 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestCallInterceptor#onConnect(RestRequest,RestResponse) onConnect(RestRequest,RestResponse)}
835 *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestCallInterceptor#onClose(RestRequest,RestResponse) onClose(RestRequest,RestResponse)}
836 *    </ul>
837 * </ul>
838 *
839 *
840 * <h4 class='topic'>Logging / Debugging</h4>
841 *
842 * <p>
843 * The following methods provide logging of requests and responses:
844 *
845 * <ul class='javatree'>
846 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
847 *    <ul>
848 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#logger(Logger) logger(Logger)}
849 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#logToConsole() logToConsole()}
850 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#logRequests(DetailLevel,Level,BiPredicate) logRequests(DetailLevel,Level,BiPredicate)}
851 *    </ul>
852 * </ul>
853 *
854 * <p>
855 * The following example shows the results of logging all requests that end with <c>/bean</c>.
856 *
857 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
858 * <p class='bjava'>
859 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = RestClient
860 *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
861 *       .json5()
862 *       .logRequests(DetailLevel.<jsf>FULL</jsf>, Level.<jsf>SEVERE</jsf>, (<jv>req</jv>,<jv>res</jv>)-&gt;<jv>req</jv>.getUri().endsWith(<js>"/bean"</js>))
863 *       .logToConsole()
864 *       .build()
865 *       .post(<js>"http://localhost/bean"</js>, <jv>anotherBean</jv>)
866 *       .run()
867 *       .getContent().as(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
868 * </p>
869 *
870 * <p>
871 * This produces the following console output:
872 *
873 * <p class='bconsole'>
874 *    === HTTP Call (outgoing) ======================================================
875 *    === REQUEST ===
876 *    POST http://localhost/bean
877 *    ---request headers---
878 *       Accept: application/json5
879 *    ---request entity---
880 *    Content-Type: application/json5
881 *    ---request content---
882 *    {f:1}
883 *    === RESPONSE ===
884 *    HTTP/1.1 200
885 *    ---response headers---
886 *       Content-Type: application/json
887 *    ---response content---
888 *    {f:1}
889 *    === END =======================================================================",
890 * </p>
891 *
892 *
893 * <p class='notes'>
894 * It should be noted that if you enable request logging detail level {@link DetailLevel#FULL}, response bodies will be cached by default which may introduce
895 * a performance penalty.
896 *
897 * <p>
898 * Additionally, the following method is also provided for enabling debug mode:
899 *
900 * <ul class='javatree'>
901 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
902 *    <ul>
903 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#debug() debug()}
904 *    </ul>
905 * </ul>
906 *
907 * <p>
908 * Enabling debug mode has the following effects:
909 * <ul>
910 *    <li>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context.Builder#debug()} is enabled.
911 *    <li>{@link Builder#detectLeaks()} is enabled.
912 *    <li>{@link Builder#logToConsole()} is called.
913 * </ul>
914 *
915 *
916 * <h4 class='topic'>REST Proxies</h4>
917 *
918 * <p>
919 * One of the more powerful features of the REST client class is the ability to produce Java interface proxies against
920 * arbitrary remote REST resources.
921 *
922 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
923 * <p class='bjava'>
924 *    <jc>// Define a Remote proxy for interacting with a REST interface.</jc>
925 *    <ja>@Remote</ja>(path=<js>"/petstore"</js>)
926 *    <jk>public interface</jk> PetStore {
927 *
928 *       <ja>@RemotePost</ja>(<js>"/pets"</js>)
929 *       Pet addPet(
930 *          <ja>@Content</ja> CreatePet <jv>pet</jv>,
931 *          <ja>@Header</ja>(<js>"E-Tag"</js>) UUID <jv>etag</jv>,
932 *          <ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"debug"</js>) <jk>boolean</jk> <jv>debug</jv>
933 *       );
934 *    }
935 *
936 *    <jc>// Use a RestClient with default JSON 5 support.</jc>
937 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json5().build())
938 *
939 *    PetStore <jv>store</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.getRemote(PetStore.<jk>class</jk>, <js>"http://localhost:10000"</js>);
940 *    CreatePet <jv>createPet</jv> = <jk>new</jk> CreatePet(<js>"Fluffy"</js>, 9.99);
941 *    Pet <jv>pet</jv> = <jv>store</jv>.addPet(<jv>createPet</jv>, UUID.<jsm>randomUUID</jsm>(), <jk>true</jk>);
942 * </p>
943 *
944 * <p>
945 * The methods to retrieve remote interfaces are:
946 *
947 * <ul class='javatree'>
948 *    <li class='jc'>{@link RestClient}
949 *    <ul>
950 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestClient#getRemote(Class) getRemote(Class&lt;T&gt;)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
951 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestClient#getRemote(Class,Object) getRemote(Class&lt;T&gt;,Object)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
952 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestClient#getRemote(Class,Object,Serializer,Parser) getRemote(Class&lt;T&gt;,Object,Serializer,Parser)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
953 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestClient#getRrpcInterface(Class) getRrpcInterface(Class&lt;T&gt;)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
954 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestClient#getRrpcInterface(Class,Object) getRrpcInterface(Class&lt;T&gt;,Object)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
955 *       <li class='jm'><c>{@link RestClient#getRrpcInterface(Class,Object,Serializer,Parser) getRrpcInterface(Class&lt;T&gt;,Object,Serializer,Parser)} <jk>returns</jk> T</c>
956 *    </ul>
957 * </ul>
958 *
959 * <p>
960 * Two basic types of remote interfaces are provided:
961 *
962 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
963 *    <li>{@link Remote @Remote}-annotated interfaces.  These can be defined against arbitrary external REST resources.
964 *    <li>RPC-over-REST interfaces.  These are Java interfaces that allow you to make method calls on server-side POJOs.
965 * </ul>
966 *
967 * <p>
968 * Refer to the following documentation on both flavors:
969 *
970 * <ul class='doctree'>
971 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestProxies">REST Proxy Basics</a>
972 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestRpc">REST/RPC</a>
973 * </ul>
974 *
975 * <br>
976 * <hr>
977 * <h4 class='topic'>Customizing Apache HttpClient</h4>
978 *
979 * <p>
980 * Several methods are provided for customizing the underlying HTTP client and client builder classes:
981 * <ul class='javatree'>
982 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
983 *    <ul>
984 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#httpClientBuilder(HttpClientBuilder) httpClientBuilder(HttpClientBuilder)} - Set the client builder yourself.
985 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#createHttpClientBuilder() createHttpClientBuilder()} - Override to create the client builder.
986 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#createHttpClient() createHttpClient()} - Override to create the client.
987 *       <li class='jm'>{@link Builder#createConnectionManager() createConnectionManager()} - Override to create the connection management.
988 *    </ul>
989 * </ul>
990 *
991 * <p>
992 * Additionally, all methods on the <c>HttpClientBuilder</c> class have been extended with fluent setters.
993 *
994 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
995 * <p class='bjava'>
996 *    <jc>// Create a client with customized HttpClient settings.</jc>
997 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = RestClient
998 *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
999 *       .disableRedirectHandling()
1000 *       .connectionManager(<jv>myConnectionManager</jv>)
1001 *       .addInterceptorFirst(<jv>myHttpRequestInterceptor</jv>)
1002 *       .build();
1003 * </p>
1004 *
1005 * <p>
1006 * Refer to the {@link HttpClientBuilder HTTP Client Builder API} for more information.
1007 *
1008 *
1009 * <h4 class='topic'>Extending RestClient</h4>
1010 *
1011 * <p>
1012 * The <c>RestClient</c> API has been designed to allow for the ability to be easily extended.
1013 * The following example that overrides the primary run method shows how this can be done.
1014 *
1015 * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
1016 * <p class='bjava'>
1017 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRestClient <jk>extends</jk> RestClient {
1018 *
1019 *       <jc>// Must provide this constructor!</jc>
1020 *       <jk>public</jk> MyRestClient(RestClient.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv>) {
1021 *          <jk>super</jk>(<jv>builder</jv>);
1022 *       }
1023 *
1024 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to customize builder settings before initialization. </jd>
1025 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1026 *       <jk>protected void</jk> init(RestClient.Builder<?>) {...}
1027 *
1028 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to provide post-initialization (e.g. setting up SAML handshakes, etc...). </jd>
1029 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1030 *       <jk>protected void</jk> init() {...}
1031 *
1032 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to customize requests when they're created (e.g. add headers to each request). </jd>
1033 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1034 *       <jk>protected</jk> RestRequest request(RestOperation) {...}
1035 *
1036 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to implement your own call handling. </jd>
1037 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1038 *       <jk>protected</jk> HttpResponse run(HttpHost, HttpRequest, HttpContext) {...}
1039 *
1040 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to customize requests before they're executed. </jd>
1041 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1042 *       <jk>protected void</jk> onCallInit(RestRequest) {...}
1043 *
1044 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to customize responses as soon as a connection is made. </jd>
1045 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1046 *       <jk>protected void</jk> onCallConnect(RestRequest, RestResponse) {...}
1047 *
1048 *       <jd>/** Optionally override to perform any call cleanup. </jd>
1049 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1050 *       <jk>protected void</jk> onCallClose(RestRequest, RestResponse) {...}
1051 *    }
1052 *
1053 *    <jc>// Instantiate your client.</jc>
1054 *    MyRestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json().build(MyRestClient.<jk>class</jk>);
1055 * </p>
1056 *
1057 * <p>
1058 * The {@link RestRequest} and {@link RestResponse} objects can also be extended and integrated by overriding the
1059 * {@link RestClient#createRequest(URI,String,boolean)} and {@link RestClient#createResponse(RestRequest,HttpResponse,Parser)} methods.
1060 *
1061 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1062 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
1063 * </ul>
1064 *
1065 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1066 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauRestClient">juneau-rest-client Basics</a>
1067 * </ul>
1068 */
1069@SuppressWarnings({
1070   "rawtypes",
1071   "resource",  // Resource management handled externally
1072   "java:S106",  // System.err is the intentional default fallback for the configurable console PrintStream
1073   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase naming convention
1074})
1075public class RestClient extends MarshallingContextable implements HttpClient, Closeable {
1076
1077   // Property name constants
1078   private static final String PROP_errorCodes = "errorCodes";
1079   private static final String PROP_executorService = "executorService";
1080   private static final String PROP_executorServiceShutdownOnClose = "executorServiceShutdownOnClose";
1081   private static final String PROP_headerData = "headerData";
1082   private static final String PROP_interceptors = "interceptors";
1083   private static final String PROP_keepHttpClientOpen = "keepHttpClientOpen";
1084   private static final String PROP_partParser = "partParser";
1085   private static final String PROP_partSerializer = "partSerializer";
1086   private static final String PROP_queryData = "queryData";
1087   private static final String PROP_rootUrl = "rootUrl";
1088
1089   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
1090   private static final String ARG_itcp = "itcp";
1091   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
1092
1093   /**
1094    * Builder class.
1095    */
1096   @SuppressWarnings({
1097      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
1098   })
1099   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends MarshallingContextable.Builder<SELF> {
1100
1101      private BeanInstantiator.Builder<RestCallHandler> callHandler;
1102      private WritableBeanStore beanStore = new BasicBeanStore();
1103      private BiPredicate<RestRequest,RestResponse> logRequestsPredicate;
1104      private boolean detectLeaks;
1105      private boolean executorServiceShutdownOnClose;
1106      private boolean ignoreErrors;
1107      private boolean keepHttpClientOpen;
1108      private boolean logToConsole;
1109      private boolean pooled;
1110      private boolean skipEmptyFormData;
1111      private boolean skipEmptyHeaderData;
1112      private boolean skipEmptyQueryData;
1113      private CloseableHttpClient httpClient;
1114      private DetailLevel logRequests;
1115      private ExecutorService executorService;
1116      private HeaderList headerData;
1117      private HttpClientBuilder httpClientBuilder;
1118      private HttpClientConnectionManager connectionManager;
1119      private HttpPartParser.Creator partParser;
1120      private HttpPartSerializer.Creator partSerializer;
1121      private Level logRequestsLevel;
1122      private Logger logger;
1123      private PartList formData;
1124      private PartList pathData;
1125      private PartList queryData;
1126      private ParserSet.Builder parsers;
1127      private Predicate<Integer> errorCodes = x -> x <= 0 || x >= 400;
1128      private PrintStream console;
1129      private SerializerSet.Builder serializers;
1130      private Supplier<String> rootUrl;
1131      private UrlEncodingSerializer.Builder urlEncodingSerializer;
1132      List<RestCallInterceptor> interceptors;
1133
1134      /**
1135       * Constructor.
1136       */
1137      protected Builder() {}
1138
1139      /**
1140       * Appends an <c>Accept</c> header on this request.
1141       *
1142       * <p>
1143       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(Accept.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>))</c>.
1144       *
1145       * @param value
1146       *    The new header value.
1147       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no Accept header will be added).
1148       * @return This object.
1149       * @see #headers()
1150       */
1151      public SELF accept(String value) {
1152         return headers(Accept.of(value));
1153      }
1154
1155      /**
1156       * Sets the value for the <c>Accept-Charset</c> request header on all requests.
1157       *
1158       * <p>
1159       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(AcceptCharset.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>))</c>.
1160       *
1161       * @param value The new header value.
1162       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no Accept-Charset header will be added).
1163       * @return This object.
1164       * @see #headers()
1165       */
1166      public SELF acceptCharset(String value) {
1167         return headers(AcceptCharset.of(value));
1168      }
1169
1170      /**
1171       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Add <js>"_type"</js> properties when needed.
1172       *
1173       * <p>
1174       * When enabled, <js>"_type"</js> properties will be added to beans if their type cannot be inferred
1175       * through reflection.
1176       *
1177       * <p>
1178       * This is used to recreate the correct objects during parsing if the object types cannot be inferred.
1179       * <br>For example, when serializing a <c>Map&lt;String,Object&gt;</c> field where the bean class cannot be determined from
1180       * the type of the values.
1181       *
1182       * <p>
1183       * Note the differences between the following settings:
1184       * <ul class='javatree'>
1185       *    <li class='jf'>{@link #addRootType()} - Affects whether <js>'_type'</js> is added to root node.
1186       *    <li class='jf'>{@link #addBeanTypes()} - Affects whether <js>'_type'</js> is added to any nodes.
1187       * </ul>
1188       *
1189       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1190       * <p class='bjava'>
1191       *    <jc>// Create a JSON client that adds _type to nodes in the request body.</jc>
1192       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1193       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1194       *       .json()
1195       *       .addBeanTypes()
1196       *       .build();
1197       *
1198       *    <jc>// Our map of beans to serialize.</jc>
1199       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"mybean"</js>)
1200       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1201       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
1202       *    }
1203       *
1204       *    AMap <jv>map</jv> = AMap.of(<js>"foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean());
1205       *
1206       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {"foo":{"_type":"mybean","foo":"bar"}}</jc>
1207       *    <jv>client</jv>
1208       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>map</jv>)
1209       *       .run();
1210       * </p>
1211       *
1212       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1213       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#addBeanTypes()}
1214       * </ul>
1215       *
1216       * @return This object.
1217       */
1218      public SELF addBeanTypes() {
1219         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::addBeanTypes);
1220         return self();
1221      }
1222
1223      /**
1224       * Adds this protocol interceptor to the head of the protocol processing list.
1225       *
1226       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1227       *    <li class='note'>This value can be overridden by the {@link #httpProcessor(HttpProcessor)} method.
1228       * </ul>
1229       *
1230       * @param itcp New property value.
1231       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1232       * @return This object.
1233       * @see HttpClientBuilder#addInterceptorFirst(HttpRequestInterceptor)
1234       */
1235      public SELF addInterceptorFirst(HttpRequestInterceptor itcp) {
1236         httpClientBuilder().addInterceptorFirst(assertArgNotNull(ARG_itcp, itcp));
1237         return self();
1238      }
1239
1240      /**
1241       * Adds this protocol interceptor to the head of the protocol processing list.
1242       *
1243       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1244       *    <li class='note'>This value can be overridden by the {@link #httpProcessor(HttpProcessor)} method.
1245       * </ul>
1246       *
1247       * @param itcp New property value.
1248       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1249       * @return This object.
1250       * @see HttpClientBuilder#addInterceptorFirst(HttpResponseInterceptor)
1251       */
1252      public SELF addInterceptorFirst(HttpResponseInterceptor itcp) {
1253         httpClientBuilder().addInterceptorFirst(assertArgNotNull(ARG_itcp, itcp));
1254         return self();
1255      }
1256
1257      /**
1258       * Adds this protocol interceptor to the tail of the protocol processing list.
1259       *
1260       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1261       *    <li class='note'>This value can be overridden by the {@link #httpProcessor(HttpProcessor)} method.
1262       * </ul>
1263       *
1264       * @param itcp New property value.
1265       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1266       * @return This object.
1267       * @see HttpClientBuilder#addInterceptorLast(HttpRequestInterceptor)
1268       */
1269      public SELF addInterceptorLast(HttpRequestInterceptor itcp) {
1270         httpClientBuilder().addInterceptorLast(assertArgNotNull(ARG_itcp, itcp));
1271         return self();
1272      }
1273
1274      /**
1275       * Adds this protocol interceptor to the tail of the protocol processing list.
1276       *
1277       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1278       *    <li class='note'>This value can be overridden by the {@link #httpProcessor(HttpProcessor)} method.
1279       * </ul>
1280       *
1281       * @param itcp New property value.
1282       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1283       * @return This object.
1284       * @see HttpClientBuilder#addInterceptorLast(HttpResponseInterceptor)
1285       */
1286      public SELF addInterceptorLast(HttpResponseInterceptor itcp) {
1287         httpClientBuilder().addInterceptorLast(assertArgNotNull(ARG_itcp, itcp));
1288         return self();
1289      }
1290
1291      /**
1292       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Add type attribute to root nodes.
1293       *
1294       * <p>
1295       * When enabled, <js>"_type"</js> properties will be added to top-level beans.
1296       *
1297       * <p>
1298       * When disabled, it is assumed that the parser knows the exact Java POJO type being parsed, and therefore top-level
1299       * type information that might normally be included to determine the data type will not be serialized.
1300       *
1301       * <p>
1302       * For example, when serializing a top-level POJO with a {@link Marshalled#typeName() @Marshalled(typeName)} value, a
1303       * <js>'_type'</js> attribute will only be added when this setting is enabled.
1304       *
1305       * <p>
1306       * Note the differences between the following settings:
1307       * <ul class='javatree'>
1308       *    <li class='jf'>{@link #addRootType()} - Affects whether <js>'_type'</js> is added to root node.
1309       *    <li class='jf'>{@link #addBeanTypes()} - Affects whether <js>'_type'</js> is added to any nodes.
1310       * </ul>
1311       *
1312       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1313       * <p class='bjava'>
1314       *    <jc>// Create a JSON client that adds _type to root node.</jc>
1315       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1316       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1317       *       .json()
1318       *       .addRootType()
1319       *       .build();
1320       *
1321       *    <jc>// Our bean to serialize.</jc>
1322       *    <ja>@Marshalled</ja>(typeName=<js>"mybean"</js>)
1323       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
1324       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
1325       *    }
1326       *
1327       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {"_type":"mybean","foo":"bar"}</jc>
1328       *    <jv>client</jv>
1329       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
1330       *       .run();
1331       * </p>
1332       *
1333       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1334       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#addRootType()}
1335       * </ul>
1336       *
1337       * @return This object.
1338       */
1339      public SELF addRootType() {
1340         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::addRootType);
1341         return self();
1342      }
1343
1344      /**
1345       * Assigns {@link BackoffManager} instance.
1346       *
1347       * @param backoffManager New property value.
1348       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, default behavior will be used).
1349       * @return This object.
1350       * @see HttpClientBuilder#setBackoffManager(BackoffManager)
1351       */
1352      public SELF backoffManager(BackoffManager backoffManager) {
1353         httpClientBuilder().setBackoffManager(backoffManager);
1354         return self();
1355      }
1356
1357      /**
1358       * Set up this client to use BASIC auth.
1359       *
1360       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1361       * <p class='bjava'>
1362       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses BASIC authentication.</jc>
1363       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1364       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1365       *       .basicAuth(<js>"http://localhost"</js>, 80, <js>"me"</js>, <js>"mypassword"</js>)
1366       *       .build();
1367       * </p>
1368       *
1369       * @param host The auth scope hostname.
1370       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use <js>"*"</js> as the hostname).
1371       * @param port The auth scope port.
1372       * @param user The username.
1373       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use empty string).
1374       * @param pw The password.
1375       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use empty string).
1376       * @return This object.
1377       */
1378      public SELF basicAuth(String host, int port, String user, String pw) {
1379         var scope = new AuthScope(host, port);
1380         var up = new UsernamePasswordCredentials(user, pw);
1381         var p = new BasicCredentialsProvider();
1382         p.setCredentials(scope, up);
1383         defaultCredentialsProvider(p);
1384         return self();
1385      }
1386
1387      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
1388      public RestClient build() {
1389         return build(RestClient.class);
1390      }
1391
1392      /**
1393       * Returns the creator for the rest call handler.
1394       *
1395       * <p>
1396       * Allows you to provide a custom handler for making HTTP calls.
1397       *
1398       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1399       * <p class='bjava'>
1400       *    <jc>// Create a client that handles processing of requests using a custom handler.</jc>
1401       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRestCallHandler <jk>implements</jk> RestCallHandler {
1402       *
1403       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
1404       *       <jk>public</jk> HttpResponse run(HttpHost <jv>target</jv>, HttpRequest <jv>request</jv>, HttpContext <jv>context</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> IOException {
1405       *          <jc>// Custom handle requests.</jc>
1406       *       }
1407       *    }
1408       *
1409       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1410       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1411       *       .callHandler(MyRestCallHandler.<jk>class</jk>)
1412       *       .build();
1413       * </p>
1414       *
1415       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1416       *    <li class='note'>
1417       *       The {@link RestClient#run(HttpHost, HttpRequest, HttpContext)} method can also be overridden to produce the same results.
1418       *    <li class='note'>
1419       *       Use {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.BeanInstantiator.Builder} <c>impl(Object)</c> to specify an already instantiated instance.
1420       *    <li class='note'>
1421       *       Use {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.BeanInstantiator.Builder} <c>type(Class)</c> to specify a subtype to instantiate.
1422       *       <br>Subclass must have a public constructor that takes in any args available
1423       *       in the bean store of this builder (including {@link RestClient} itself).
1424       * </ul>
1425       *
1426       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1427       *    <li class='jic'>{@link RestCallHandler}
1428       * </ul>
1429       *
1430       * @return The instantiator for the rest call handler.
1431       */
1432      public final BeanInstantiator.Builder<RestCallHandler> callHandler() {
1433         if (callHandler == null)
1434            callHandler = createCallHandler();
1435         return callHandler;
1436      }
1437
1438      /**
1439       * REST call handler class.
1440       *
1441       * <p>
1442       * Specifies a custom handler for making HTTP calls.
1443       *
1444       * <p>
1445       * This is a shortcut for <c>callHandler().type(<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
1446       *
1447       * @param value
1448       *    The new value for this setting.
1449       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1450       * @return This object.
1451       * @see #callHandler()
1452       */
1453      public SELF callHandler(Class<? extends RestCallHandler> value) {
1454         callHandler().type(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
1455         return self();
1456      }
1457
1458      /**
1459       * Sets the client version by setting the value for the <js>"Client-Version"</js> header.
1460       *
1461       * <p>
1462       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(ClientVersion.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>))</c>.
1463       *
1464       * @param value The version string (e.g. <js>"1.2.3"</js>)
1465       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no Client-Version header will be added).
1466       * @return This object.
1467       * @see #headers()
1468       */
1469      public SELF clientVersion(String value) {
1470         return headers(ClientVersion.of(value));
1471      }
1472
1473      /**
1474       * Assigns {@link ConnectionBackoffStrategy} instance.
1475       *
1476       * @param connectionBackoffStrategy New property value.
1477       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, default behavior will be used).
1478       * @return This object.

1615      /**
1616       * <i><l>Parser</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Debug output lines.
1617       *
1618       * <p>
1619       * When parse errors occur, this specifies the number of lines of input before and after the
1620       * error location to be printed as part of the exception message.
1621       *
1622       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1623       * <p class='bjava'>
1624       *    <jc>// Create a parser whose exceptions print out 100 lines before and after the parse error location.</jc>
1625       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1626       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1627       *       .json()
1628       *       .debug()  <jc>// Enable debug mode to capture Reader contents as strings.</jc>
1629       *       .debugOuputLines(100)
1630       *       .build();
1631       *
1632       *    <jc>// Try to parse some bad JSON.</jc>
1633       *    <jk>try</jk> {
1634       *       <jv>client</jv>
1635       *          .get(<js>"/pathToBadJson"</js>)
1636       *          .run()
1637       *          .getContent().as(Object.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// Try to parse it.</jc>
1638       *    } <jk>catch</jk> (RestCallException <jv>e</jv>) {
1639       *       System.<jsf>err</jsf>.println(<jv>e</jv>.getMessage());  <jc>// Will display 200 lines of the output.</jc>
1640       *    }
1641       * </p>
1642       *
1643       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1644       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.Parser.Builder#debugOutputLines(int)}
1645       * </ul>
1646       *
1647       * @param value
1648       *    The new value for this property.
1649       *    <br>The default value is <c>5</c>.
1650       * @return This object.
1651       */
1652      public SELF debugOutputLines(int value) {
1653         parsers().forEach(x -> x.debugOutputLines(value));
1654         return self();
1655      }

1756      /**
1757       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Enable leak detection.
1758       *
1759       * <p>
1760       * Enable client and request/response leak detection.
1761       *
1762       * <p>
1763       * Causes messages to be logged to the console if clients or request/response objects are not properly closed
1764       * when the <c>finalize</c> methods are invoked.
1765       *
1766       * <p>
1767       * Automatically enabled with {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context.Builder#debug()}.
1768       *
1769       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1770       * <p class='bjava'>
1771       *    <jc>// Create a client that logs a message if </jc>
1772       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1773       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1774       *       .detectLeaks()
1775       *       .logToConsole()  <jc>// Also log the error message to System.err</jc>
1776       *       .build();
1777       *
1778       *    <jv>client</jv>.closeQuietly();  <jc>// Customized HttpClient won't be closed.</jc>
1779       * </p>
1780       *
1781       * @return This object.
1782       */
1783      public SELF detectLeaks() {
1784         detectLeaks = true;
1785         return self();
1786      }
1787
1788      /**
1789       * <i><l>BeanTraverse</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Automatically detect POJO recursions.
1790       *
1791       * <p>
1792       * When enabled, specifies that recursions should be checked for during traversal.
1793       *
1794       * <p>
1795       * Recursions can occur when traversing models that aren't true trees but rather contain loops.
1796       * <br>In general, unchecked recursions cause stack-overflow-errors.
1797       * <br>These show up as {@link MarshallingRecursionException MarshallingRecursionException} with the message <js>"Depth too deep.  Stack overflow occurred."</js>.
1798       *
1799       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
1800       *    <li class='note'>
1801       *       Checking for recursion can cause a small performance penalty.
1802       * </ul>
1803       *
1804       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
1805       * <p class='bjava'>
1806       *    <jc>// Create a JSON client that automatically checks for recursions.</jc>
1807       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
1808       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
1809       *       .json()
1810       *       .detectRecursions()
1811       *       .build();
1812       *
1813       *    <jc>// Create a POJO model with a recursive loop.</jc>
1814       *    <jk>public class</jk> A {
1815       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>f</jf>;
1816       *    }
1817       *    A <jv>a</jv> = <jk>new</jk> A();
1818       *    <jv>a</jv>.<jf>f</jf> = <jv>a</jv>;
1819       *
1820       * <jk>try</jk> {
1821       *       <jc>// Throws a RestCallException with an inner SerializeException and not a StackOverflowError</jc>
1822       *       <jv>client</jv>
1823       *          .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>a</jv>)
1824       *          .run();
1825       * } <jk>catch</jk> (RestCallException <jv>e</jv>} {
1826       *    <jc>// Handle exception.</jc>
1827       * }
1828       * </p>
1829       *
1830       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
1831       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext.Builder#detectRecursions()}
1832       * </ul>
1833       *
1834       * @return This object.
1835       */
1836      public SELF detectRecursions() {
1837         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::detectRecursions);
1838         return self();
1839      }
1840

1945      /**

1950       *    <li class='note'>This method has no effect if the instance of {@link HttpClient} is configured to use a shared connection manager.
1951       *    <li class='note'>This method may not be used when the instance of {@link HttpClient} is created inside an EJB container.
1952       * </ul>

1955       * @see HttpClientBuilder#evictExpiredConnections()
1956       */

1967       *    <li class='note'>This method has no effect if the instance of {@link HttpClient} is configured to use a shared connection manager.
1968       *    <li class='note'>This method may not be used when the instance of {@link HttpClient} is created inside an EJB container.
1969       * </ul>
1970       *
1971       * @param maxIdleTime New property value.
1972       * @param maxIdleTimeUnit New property value.
1973       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
1974       * @return This object.

1976       */

1978         httpClientBuilder().evictIdleConnections(maxIdleTime, maxIdleTimeUnit);
1979         return self();

1982      /**
1983       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Executor service.
1984       *
1985       * <p>
1986       * Defines the executor service to use when calling future methods on the {@link RestRequest} class.
1987       *
1988       * <p>
1989       * This executor service is used to create {@link Future} objects on the following methods:
1990       * <ul>
1991       *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#runFuture()}
1992       *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#completeFuture()}
1993       *    <li class='jm'>{@link ResponseContent#asFuture(Class)} (and similar methods)
1994       * </ul>
1995       *
1996       * <p>
1997       * The default executor service is a single-threaded {@link ThreadPoolExecutor} with a 30 second timeout
1998       * and a queue size of 10.
1999       *
2000       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2001       * <p class='bjava'>
2002       *    <jc>// Create a client with a customized executor service.</jc>
2003       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2004       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2005       *       .executorService(<jk>new</jk> ThreadPoolExecutor(1, 1, 30, TimeUnit.<jsf>SECONDS</jsf>, <jk>new</jk> ArrayBlockingQueue&lt;Runnable&gt;(10)), <jk>true</jk>)
2006       *       .build();
2007       *
2008       *    <jc>// Use it to asynchronously run a request.</jc>
2009       *    Future&lt;RestResponse&gt; <jv>responseFuture</jv> = <jv>client</jv>.get(<jsf>URI</jsf>).runFuture();
2010       *
2011       *    <jc>// Do some other stuff.</jc>
2012       *
2013       *    <jc>// Now read the response.</jc>
2014       *    String <jv>body</jv> = <jv>responseFuture</jv>.get().getContent().asString();
2015       *
2016       *    <jc>// Use it to asynchronously retrieve a response.</jc>
2017       *    Future&lt;MyBean&gt; <jv>myBeanFuture</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
2018       *       .get(<jsf>URI</jsf>)
2019       *       .run()
2020       *       .getContent().asFuture(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
2021       *
2022       *    <jc>// Do some other stuff.</jc>
2023       *
2024       *    <jc>// Now read the response.</jc>
2025       *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>myBeanFuture</jv>.get();
2026       * </p>
2027       *
2028       * @param executorService The executor service.
2029       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (a default executor service will be created if needed).
2030       * @param shutdownOnClose Call {@link ExecutorService#shutdown()} when {@link RestClient#close()} is called.
2031       * @return This object.
2032       */
2033      public SELF executorService(ExecutorService executorService, boolean shutdownOnClose) {
2034         this.executorService = executorService;
2035         this.executorServiceShutdownOnClose = shutdownOnClose;
2036         return self();
2037      }
2038
2039      /**
2040       * Returns the builder for the list of form data parameters that get applied to all requests created by this builder.
2041       *
2042       * <p>
2043       * This is the primary method for accessing the form data parameter list.
2044       * On first call, the builder is created via the method {@link #createFormData()}.
2045       *
2046       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2047       * <p class='bjava'>
2048       *    <jc>// Create a client that adds a "foo=bar" form-data parameter on every request.</jc>
2049       *    RestClient.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>();
2050       *    <jv>builder</jv>.formData().setDefault(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>));
2051       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jv>builder</jv>.build();
2052       * </p>
2053       *
2054       * <p>
2055       * The following convenience methods are also provided for updating the parameters:
2056       * <ul>
2057       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #formData(NameValuePair...)}
2058       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #formDataDefault(NameValuePair...)}
2059       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #formData(String,String)}
2060       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #formData(String,Supplier)}
2061       * </ul>
2062       *
2063       * @return The form data list builder.
2064       */
2065      public final PartList formData() {
2066         if (formData == null)
2067            formData = createFormData();
2068         return formData;
2069      }
2070
2071      /**
2072       * Appends multiple form-data parameters to the request bodies of all URL-encoded form posts.
2073       *
2074       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2075       * <p class='bjava'>
2076       *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.http.HttpParts.*;
2077       *
2078       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2079       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2080       *       .formData(
2081       *          <jsm>stringPart</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
2082       *          <jsm>booleanPart</jsm>(<js>"baz"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
2083       *       )
2084       *       .build();
2085       * </p>
2086       *
2087       * <p>
2088       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>formData().append(<jv>parts</jv>)</c>.
2089       *
2090       * @param parts
2091       *    The form-data parameters.
2092       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
2093       * @return This object.
2094       * @see #formData()
2095       */
2096      public SELF formData(NameValuePair...parts) {
2097         formData().append(parts);
2098         return self();
2099      }
2100
2101      /**
2102       * Appends a form-data parameter to all request bodies.
2103       *
2104       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2105       * <p class='bjava'>
2106       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2107       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2108       *       .formData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
2109       *       .build();
2110       * </p>
2111       *
2112       * <p>
2113       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>formData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
2114       *
2115       * @param name The parameter name.
2116       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2117       * @param value The parameter value.
2118       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (null value will be serialized).
2119       * @return This object.
2120       * @see #formData()
2121       */
2122      public SELF formData(String name, String value) {
2123         formData().append(name, value);
2124         return self();
2125      }

2127      /**
2128       * Appends a form-data parameter with a dynamic value to all request bodies.
2129       *
2130       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2131       * <p class='bjava'>
2132       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2133       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2134       *       .formData(<js>"foo"</js>, ()-&gt;<js>"bar"</js>)
2135       *       .build();
2136       * </p>
2137       *
2138       * <p>
2139       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>formData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
2140       *
2141       * @param name The parameter name.
2142       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
2143       * @param value The parameter value supplier.
2144       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (null value will be serialized).
2145       * @return This object.
2146       * @see #formData()
2147       */
2148      public SELF formData(String name, Supplier<String> value) {
2149         formData().append(name, value);
2150         return self();
2151      }
2152
2153      /**
2154       * Sets default form-data parameter values.
2155       *
2156       * <p>
2157       * Uses default values for specified parameters if not otherwise specified on the outgoing requests.
2158       *
2159       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2160       * <p class='bjava'>
2161       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2162       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()

2164       *       .build();
2165       * </p>
2166       *
2167       * <p>
2168       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>formData().setDefault(<jv>parts</jv>)</c>.
2169       *
2170       * @param parts The parts.
2171       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
2172       * @return This object.
2173       * @see #formData()
2174       */
2175      public SELF formDataDefault(NameValuePair...parts) {
2176         formData().setDefault(parts);
2177         return self();
