2180      /**
2181       * Returns the root URI defined for this client.
2182       *
2183       * <p>
2184       * Returns <jk>null</jk> in leu of an empty string.
2185       * Trailing slashes are trimmed.
2186       *
2187       * @return The root URI defined for this client.
2188       */
2189      public String getRootUri() { return rootUrl != null ? rootUrl.get() : null; }
2190
2191      /**
2192       * Returns the root URL supplier set on this builder.
2193       *
2194       * @return The root URL supplier, or <jk>null</jk> if not set.
2195       */
2196      public Supplier<String> getRootUrlSupplier() { return rootUrl; }

2204       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()

2206       *       .build();
2207       * </p>

2209       * <p>
2210       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.

2212       * @param name The header name.
2213       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.

2216       * @return This object.
2217       * @see #headers()
2218       */
2219      public SELF header(String name, String value) {
2220         headers().append(name, value);
2221         return self();
2222      }

2224      /**
2225       * Appends a header to all requests using a dynamic value.

2230       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()

2232       *       .build();
2233       * </p>
2234       *

2236       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
2237       *
2238       * @param name The header name.
2239       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.

2250      /**
2251       * Returns the builder for the list of headers that get applied to all requests created by this builder.
2252       *
2253       * <p>
2254       * This is the primary method for accessing the request header list.
2255       * On first call, the builder is created via the method {@link #createHeaderData()}.
2256       *
2257       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2258       * <p class='bjava'>
2259       *    <jc>// Create a client that adds a "Foo: bar" header on every request.</jc>
2260       *    RestClient.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>();
2261       *    <jv>builder</jv>.headerData().setDefault(<js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>));
2262       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jv>builder</jv>.build();
2263       * </p>
2264       *
2265       * <p>
2266       * The following convenience methods are also provided for updating the headers:
2267       * <ul>
2268       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #headers(Header...)}
2269       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #headersDefault(Header...)}
2270       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #header(String,String)}
2271       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #header(String,Supplier)}
2272       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #mediaType(String)}
2273       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #mediaType(MediaType)}
2274       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #accept(String)}
2275       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #acceptCharset(String)}
2276       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #clientVersion(String)}
2277       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #contentType(String)}
2278       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #debug()}
2279       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #noTrace()}
2280       * </ul>
2281       *
2282       * @return The header list builder.
2283       */
2284      public final HeaderList headers() {
2285         if (headerData == null)
2286            headerData = createHeaderData();
2287         return headerData;
2288      }
2289
2290      /**
2291       * Appends multiple headers to all requests.
2292       *
2293       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2294       * <p class='bjava'>
2295       *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders.*;
2296       *
2297       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2298       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2299       *       .headers(
2300       *          <jsf>ACCEPT_TEXT_XML</jsf>,
2301       *          <jsm>stringHeader</jsm>(<js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
2302       *       )
2303       *       .build();
2304       * </p>
2305       *
2306       * <p>
2307       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(<jv>parts</jv>)</c>.
2308       *
2309       * @param parts
2310       *    The header to set.
2311       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
2312       * @return This object.
2313       * @see #headers()
2314       */
2315      public SELF headers(Header...parts) {
2316         headers().append(parts);
2317         return self();
2318      }

2320      /**
2321       * Sets default header values.

2323       * <p>
2324       * Uses default values for specified headers if not otherwise specified on the outgoing requests.
2325       *
2326       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2327       * <p class='bjava'>
2328       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2329       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()

2342      public SELF headersDefault(Header...parts) {

2347      /**
2348       * Sets a default request header with serializer session options (JSON5 map) for all requests.
2349       *
2350       * <p>
2351       * This is a shortcut for <c>headersDefault(basicHeader({@link RestSharedConstants#HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions}, <jv>json5</jv>))</c>.
2352       *
2353       * @param json5
2354       *    Serializer session options in JSON5 form.
2355       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (default is not set by this method).
2356       * @return This object.
2357       */
2358      public SELF serializerSessionOptionsHeader(String json5) {
2359         if (json5 != null)
2360            headersDefault(basicHeader(HEADER_JuneauSerializerOptions, json5));
2361         return self();
2362      }
2363
2364      /**
2365       * Sets a default request header with parser session options (JSON5 map) for all requests.
2366       *
2367       * <p>
2368       * This is a shortcut for <c>headersDefault(basicHeader({@link RestSharedConstants#HEADER_JuneauParserOptions}, <jv>json5</jv>))</c>.
2369       *
2370       * @param json5
2371       *    Parser session options in JSON5 form.
2372       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (default is not set by this method).
2373       * @return This object.
2374       */
2375      public SELF parserSessionOptionsHeader(String json5) {
2376         if (json5 != null)
2377            headersDefault(basicHeader(HEADER_JuneauParserOptions, json5));
2378         return self();
2379      }
2380
2381      /**
2382       * Convenience for {@link #serializerSessionOptionsHeader(String)} using a map serialized with JSON5.
2383       *
2384       * @param properties
2385       *    Property names and values for serializer session options.
2386       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as an empty map).
2387       * @return This object.
2388       */
2389      public SELF serializerSessionOptionsHeader(Map<String,?> properties) {
2390         try {
2391            return serializerSessionOptionsHeader(isEmpty(properties) ? null : Json5.of(properties));
2392         } catch (SerializeException e) {
2393            throw rex(e, "Could not serialize serializer session options header");
2394         }
2395      }
2396
2397      /**
2398       * Convenience for {@link #parserSessionOptionsHeader(String)} using a map serialized with JSON5.
2399       *
2400       * @param properties
2401       *    Property names and values for parser session options.
2402       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as an empty map).
2403       * @return This object.
2404       */
2405      public SELF parserSessionOptionsHeader(Map<String,?> properties) {
2406         try {
2407            return parserSessionOptionsHeader(isEmpty(properties) ? null : Json5.of(properties));
2408         } catch (SerializeException e) {
2409            throw rex(e, "Could not serialize parser session options header");
2410         }
2411      }
2412
2413      /**
2414       * Convenience method for specifying HTML as the marshalling transmission media type.
2415       *
2416       * <p>
2417       * POJOs are converted to HTML without any sort of doc wrappers.
2418       *
2419       * <p>
2420       *    {@link HtmlSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
2421       *    <ul>
2422       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
2423       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2424       *    </ul>
2425       * <p>
2426       *    {@link HtmlParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
2427       *    <ul>
2428       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
2429       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2430       *    </ul>
2431       * <p>
2432       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
2433       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2434       * <p>
2435       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
2436       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2437       * <p>
2438       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
2439       *    <ul>
2440       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
2441       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
2442       *    </ul>
2443       * <p>
2444       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(HtmlSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2445       *
2446       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2447       * <p class='bjava'>
2448       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses HTML marshalling.</jc>
2449       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().html().build();
2450       * </p>
2451       *
2452       * @return This object.
2453       */
2454      public SELF html() {
2455         return serializer(HtmlSerializer.class).parser(HtmlParser.class);
2456      }
2457
2458      /**
2459       * Convenience method for specifying HTML DOC as the marshalling transmission media type.
2460       *
2461       * <p>
2462       * POJOs are converted to fully renderable HTML pages.
2463       *
2464       * <p>
2465       *    {@link HtmlDocSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
2466       *    <ul>
2467       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()} or
2468       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2469       *    </ul>
2470       * <p>
2471       *    {@link HtmlParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
2472       *    <ul>
2473       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
2474       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2475       *    </ul>
2476       * <p>
2477       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
2478       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2479       * <p>
2480       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html"</js> unless overridden
2481       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2482       * <p>
2483       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
2484       *    <ul>
2485       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
2486       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
2487       *    </ul>
2488       * <p>
2489       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(HtmlDocSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2490       *
2491       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2492       * <p class='bjava'>
2493       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses HTML Doc marshalling.</jc>
2494       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().htmlDoc().build();
2495       * </p>
2496       *
2497       * @return This object.
2498       */
2499      public SELF htmlDoc() {
2500         return serializer(HtmlDocSerializer.class).parser(HtmlParser.class);
2501      }

2519       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2520       *    </ul>
2521       * <p>

2525       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/html+stripped"</js> unless overridden
2526       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.

2528       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
2529       *    <ul>
2530       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
2531       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
2532       *    </ul>
2533       * <p>

2536       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2537       * <p class='bjava'>
2538       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses HTML Stripped Doc marshalling.</jc>
2539       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().htmlStrippedDoc().build();
2540       * </p>
2541       *

2548      /**
2549       * Sets the {@link HttpClient} to be used to handle all HTTP communications with the target server.

2558       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()

2560       *       .build();

2563       * @param value The {@link HttpClient} to be used to handle all HTTP communications with the target server.
2564       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (a default client will be created).
2565       * @return This object.
2566       */
2567      public SELF httpClient(CloseableHttpClient value) {
2568         httpClient = value;
2569         return self();

2573       * Returns the HTTP client builder.

2575       * @return The HTTP client builder.

2579            httpClientBuilder = createHttpClientBuilder();

2583      /**
2584       * Sets the {@link HttpClientBuilder} that will be used to create the {@link HttpClient} used by {@link RestClient}.
2585       *
2586       * <p>
2587       * This can be used to bypass the builder created by {@link #createHttpClientBuilder()} method.
2588       *
2589       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2590       * <p class='bjava'>
2591       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses a customized HttpClientBuilder.</jc>
2592       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2593       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2594       *       .httpClientBuilder(HttpClientBuilder.<jsm>create</jsm>())
2595       *       .build();
2596       * </p>
2597       *
2598       * @param value The {@link HttpClientBuilder} that will be used to create the {@link HttpClient} used by {@link RestClient}.
2599       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (a default builder will be created).
2600       * @return This object.
2601       */
2602      public SELF httpClientBuilder(HttpClientBuilder value) {
2603         httpClientBuilder = value;
2604         return self();
2605      }
2606
2607      /**
2608       * Assigns {@link HttpProcessor} instance.
2609       *
2610       * @param httpprocessor New property value.
2611       * @return This object.
2612       * @see HttpClientBuilder#setHttpProcessor(HttpProcessor)
2613       */
2614      public SELF httpProcessor(HttpProcessor httpprocessor) {
2615         httpClientBuilder().setHttpProcessor(httpprocessor);
2616         return self();
2617      }
2618
2619      /**
2620       * Ignore errors.
2621       *
2622       * <p>
2623       * When enabled, HTTP error response codes (e.g. <l>&gt;=400</l>) will not cause a {@link RestCallException} to
2624       * be thrown.
2625       * <p>
2626       * Note that this is equivalent to <c>builder.errorCodes(x -&gt; <jk>false</jk>);</c>
2627       *
2628       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2629       * <p class='bjava'>
2630       *    <jc>// Create a client that doesn't throws a RestCallException when a 500 error occurs.</jc>
2631       *    RestClient
2632       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2633       *       .ignoreErrors()
2634       *       .build()
2635       *       .get(<js>"/error"</js>)  <jc>// Throws a 500 error</jc>
2636       *       .run()
2637       *       .assertStatus().is(500);
2638       * </p>
2639       *
2640       * @return This object.
2641       */
2642      public SELF ignoreErrors() {
2643         ignoreErrors = true;
2644         return self();
2645      }
2646
2647      /**
2648       * <i><l>BeanTraverse</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Ignore recursion errors.
2649       *
2650       * <p>
2651       * When enabled, when we encounter the same object when traversing a tree, we set the value to <jk>null</jk>.
2652       *
2653       * <p>
2654       * For example, if a model contains the links A-&gt;B-&gt;C-&gt;A, then the JSON generated will look like
2655       *    the following when <jsf>BEANTRAVERSE_ignoreRecursions</jsf> is <jk>true</jk>...
2656       *
2657       * <p class='bjson'>
2658       *    {A:{B:{C:<jk>null</jk>}}}
2659       * </p>
2660       *
2661       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2662       *    <li class='note'>
2663       *       Checking for recursion can cause a small performance penalty.
2664       * </ul>
2665       *
2666       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2667       * <p class='bjava'>
2668       *    <jc>// Create a JSON client that ignores recursions.</jc>
2669       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2670       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2671       *       .json()
2672       *       .ignoreRecursions()
2673       *       .build();
2674       *
2675       *    <jc>// Create a POJO model with a recursive loop.</jc>
2676       *    <jk>public class</jk> A {
2677       *       <jk>public</jk> Object <jf>f</jf>;
2678       *    }
2679       *    A <jv>a</jv> = <jk>new</jk> A();
2680       *    <jv>a</jv>.<jf>f</jf> = <jv>a</jv>;
2681       *
2682       *    <jc>// Produces request body "{f:null}"</jc>
2683       *    <jv>client</jv>
2684       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>a</jv>)
2685       *       .run();
2686       * </p>
2687       *
2688       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2689       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext.Builder#ignoreRecursions()}
2690       * </ul>
2691       *
2692       * @return This object.
2693       */
2694      public SELF ignoreRecursions() {
2695         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::ignoreRecursions);
2696         return self();
2697      }
2698
2699      /**
2700       * <i><l>BeanTraverse</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Initial depth.
2701       *
2702       * <p>
2703       * The initial indentation level at the root.
2704       *
2705       * <p>
2706       * Useful when constructing document fragments that need to be indented at a certain level when whitespace is enabled.
2707       *
2708       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2709       * <p class='bjava'>
2710       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer with whitespace enabled and an initial depth of 2.</jc>
2711       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2712       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2713       *       .json()
2714       *       .ws()
2715       *       .initialDepth(2)
2716       *       .build();
2717       *
2718       *    <jc>// Our bean to serialize.</jc>
2719       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
2720       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <jk>null</jk>;
2721       *    }
2722       *
2723       *    <jc>// Produces request body "\t\t{\n\t\t\t'foo':'bar'\n\t\t}\n"</jc>
2724       *    <jv>client</jv>
2725       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
2726       *       .run();
2727       * </p>
2728       *
2729       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
2730       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext.Builder#initialDepth(int)}
2731       * </ul>
2732       *
2733       * @param value
2734       *    The new value for this property.
2735       *    <br>The default is <c>0</c>.
2736       * @return This object.
2737       */
2738      public SELF initialDepth(int value) {
2739         serializers().forEach(x -> x.initialDepth(value));
2740         return self();
2741      }
2742
2743      /**
2744       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Call interceptors.
2745       *
2746       * <p>
2747       * Adds an interceptor that can be called to hook into specified events in the lifecycle of a single request.
2748       *
2749       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2750       * <p class='bjava'>
2751       *   <jc>// Customized interceptor (note you can also extend from BasicRestCallInterceptor as well.</jc>
2752       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyRestCallInterceptor <jk>implements</jk> RestCallInterceptor {
2753       *
2754       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
2755       *       <jk>public void</jk> onInit(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2756       *       <jc>// Intercept immediately after RestRequest object is created and all headers/query/form-data has been
2757       *       // set on the request from the client.</jc>
2758       *    }
2759       *
2760       *    <ja>@Override</ja>
2761       *    <jk>public void</jk> onConnect(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, RestResponse <jv>res</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2762       *       <jc>// Intercept immediately after an HTTP response has been received.</jc>
2763       *    }
2764       *
2765       *    <ja>@Override</ja>
2766       *    <jk>public void</jk> onClose(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, RestResponse <jv>res</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2767       *          <jc>// Intercept when the response body is consumed.</jc>
2768       *       }
2769       *    }
2770       *
2771       *    <jc>// Create a client with a customized interceptor.</jc>
2772       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2773       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2774       *       .interceptors(MyRestCallInterceptor.<jk>class</jk>)
2775       *       .build();
2776       * </p>
2777       *
2778       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2779       *    <li class='note'>The {@link RestClient#onCallInit(RestRequest)}, {@link RestClient#onCallConnect(RestRequest,RestResponse)}, and
2780       * {@link RestClient#onCallClose(RestRequest,RestResponse)} methods can also be overridden to produce the same results.
2781       * </ul>
2782       *
2783       * @param values
2784       *    The values to add to this setting.
2785       *    <br>Can be implementations of any of the following:
2786       *    <ul>
2787       *       <li class='jic'>{@link RestCallInterceptor}
2788       *       <li class='jic'>{@link HttpRequestInterceptor}
2789       *       <li class='jic'>{@link HttpResponseInterceptor}
2790       *    </ul>
2791       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
2792       * @return This object.
2793       * @throws Exception If one or more interceptors could not be created.
2794       */
2795      @SuppressWarnings({
2796         "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
2797      })
2798      public SELF interceptors(Class<?>...values) throws Exception {
2799         for (var c : values) {
2800            if (c == null)
2801               continue;
2802            var ci = ClassInfo.of(c);
2803            if (nn(ci)) {
2804               if (ci.isAssignableToAny(RestCallInterceptor.class, HttpRequestInterceptor.class, HttpResponseInterceptor.class))
2805                  interceptors(ci.newInstance());
2806               else
2807                  throw new ConfigException("Invalid class of type ''{0}'' passed to interceptors().", ci.getName());
2808            }
2809         }
2810         return self();
2811      }
2812
2813      /**
2814       * Call interceptors.
2815       *
2816       * <p>
2817       * Adds an interceptor that gets called immediately after a connection is made.
2818       *
2819       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2820       * <p class='bjava'>
2821       *    <jc>// Create a client with a customized interceptor.</jc>
2822       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
2823       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
2824       *       .interceptors(
2825       *          <jk>new</jk> RestCallInterceptor() {
2826       *
2827       *             <ja>@Override</ja>
2828       *             <jk>public void</jk> onInit(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2829       *             <jc>// Intercept immediately after RestRequest object is created and all headers/query/form-data has been
2830       *             // set on the request from the client.</jc>
2831       *          }
2832       *
2833       *          <ja>@Override</ja>
2834       *          <jk>public void</jk> onConnect(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, RestResponse <jv>res</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2835       *             <jc>// Intercept immediately after an HTTP response has been received.</jc>
2836       *          }
2837       *
2838       *          <ja>@Override</ja>
2839       *          <jk>public void</jk> onClose(RestRequest <jv>req</jv>, RestResponse <jv>res</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
2840       *                <jc>// Intercept when the response body is consumed.</jc>
2841       *             }
2842       *          }
2843       *       )
2844       *       .build();
2845       * </p>
2846       *
2847       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
2848       *    <li class='note'>The {@link RestClient#onCallInit(RestRequest)}, {@link RestClient#onCallConnect(RestRequest,RestResponse)}, and
2849       * {@link RestClient#onCallClose(RestRequest,RestResponse)} methods can also be overridden to produce the same results.
2850       * </ul>
2851       *
2852       * @param value
2853       *    The values to add to this setting.
2854       *    <br>Can be implementations of any of the following:
2855       *    <ul>
2856       *       <li class='jic'>{@link RestCallInterceptor}
2857       *       <li class='jic'>{@link HttpRequestInterceptor}
2858       *       <li class='jic'>{@link HttpResponseInterceptor}
2859       *    </ul>
2860       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
2861       * @return This object.
2862       */
2863      @SuppressWarnings({
2864         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for interceptor configuration
2865      })
2866      public SELF interceptors(Object...value) {
2867         List<RestCallInterceptor> l = list();
2868         for (var o : value) {
2869            if (o == null)
2870               continue;
2871            ClassInfo ci = ClassInfo.of(o);
2872            if (nn(ci)) {
2873               if (! ci.isAssignableToAny(HttpRequestInterceptor.class, HttpResponseInterceptor.class, RestCallInterceptor.class))
2874                  throw new ConfigException("Invalid object of type ''{0}'' passed to interceptors().", ci.getName());
2875               if (o instanceof HttpRequestInterceptor o2)
2876                  addInterceptorLast(o2);
2877               if (o instanceof HttpResponseInterceptor o2)
2878                  addInterceptorLast(o2);
2879               if (o instanceof RestCallInterceptor o2)
2880                  l.add(o2);
2881            }
2882         }
2883         if (interceptors == null)
2884            interceptors = l;
2885         else
2886            interceptors.addAll(0, l);
2887         return self();
2888      }
2889
2890      /**
2891       * Convenience method for specifying JSON as the marshalling transmission media type.
2892       *
2893       * <p>
2894       * {@link JsonSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
2895       *    <ul>
2896       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
2897       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2898       *    </ul>
2899       * <p>
2900       *    {@link JsonParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
2901       *    <ul>
2902       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
2903       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2904       *    </ul>
2905       * <p>
2906       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json"</js> unless overridden
2907       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}}.
2908       * <p>
2909       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json"</js> unless overridden
2910       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2911       * <p>
2912       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #xml()} to provide support for multiple languages.
2913       *    <ul>
2914       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
2915       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
2916       *    </ul>
2917       * <p>
2918       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(JsonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2919       *
2920       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2921       * <p class='bjava'>
2922       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses JSON marshalling.</jc>
2923       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json().build();
2924       * </p>
2925       *
2926       * @return This object.
2927       */
2928      public SELF json() {
2929         return serializer(JsonSerializer.class).parser(JsonParser.class);
2930      }
2931
2932      /**
2933       * Convenience method for specifying Simplified JSON as the marshalling transmission media type.
2934       *
2935       * <p>
2936       * Simplified JSON is typically useful for automated tests because you can do simple string comparison of results
2937       * without having to escape lots of quotes.
2938       *
2939       * <p>
2940       *    {@link Json5Serializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
2941       *    <ul>
2942       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
2943       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2944       *    </ul>
2945       * <p>
2946       *    {@link Json5Parser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
2947       *    <ul>
2948       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
2949       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
2950       *    </ul>
2951       * <p>
2952       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json"</js> unless overridden
2953       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2954       * <p>
2955       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/json5"</js> unless overridden
2956       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
2957       * <p>
2958       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #xml()} to provide support for multiple languages.
2959       *    <ul>
2960       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
2961       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
2962       *    </ul>
2963       * <p>
2964       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(Json5Serializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(Json5Parser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2965       *
2966       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
2967       * <p class='bjava'>
2968       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses Simplified JSON marshalling.</jc>
2969       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().json5().build();
2970       * </p>
2971       *
2972       * @return This object.
2973       */
2974      public SELF json5() {
2975         return serializer(Json5Serializer.class).parser(Json5Parser.class);
2976      }
2977
2978      /**
2979       * Convenience method for specifying JCS (JSON Canonicalization Scheme, RFC 8785) as the marshalling transmission media type.
2980       *
2981       * <p>
2982       *    {@link JcsSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies.
2983       *    {@link JsonParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies (JCS output is valid JSON).
2984       * <p>
2985       *    <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> will be set to <js>"application/jcs+json"</js>.
2986       * <p>
2987       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(JcsSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
2988       *
2989       * @return This object.
2990       */
2991      public SELF jcs() {
2992         return serializer(JcsSerializer.class).parser(JsonParser.class);
2993      }
2994
2995      /**
2996       * Assigns {@link ConnectionKeepAliveStrategy} instance.
2997       *
2998       * @param keepAliveStrategy New property value.
2999       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, default behavior will be used).
3000       * @return This object.
3001       * @see HttpClientBuilder#setKeepAliveStrategy(ConnectionKeepAliveStrategy)
3002       */
3003      public SELF keepAliveStrategy(ConnectionKeepAliveStrategy keepAliveStrategy) {
3004         httpClientBuilder().setKeepAliveStrategy(keepAliveStrategy);
3005         return self();

3008      /**
3009       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Keep HttpClient open.
3010       *
3011       * <p>
3012       * Don't close this client when the {@link RestClient#close()} method is called.
3013       *
3014       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3015       * <p class='bjava'>
3016       *    <jc>// Create a client with a customized client and don't close the client  service.</jc>
3017       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3018       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3019       *       .httpClient(<jv>myHttpClient</jv>)
3020       *       .keepHttpClientOpen()
3021       *       .build();
3022       *
3023       *    <jv>client</jv>.closeQuietly();  <jc>// Customized HttpClient won't be closed.</jc>
3024       * </p>
3025       *
3026       * @return This object.
3027       */
3028      public SELF keepHttpClientOpen() {
3029         keepHttpClientOpen = true;
3030         return self();
3031      }

3033      /**
3034       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Don't trim null bean property values.
3035       *
3036       * <p>
3037       * When enabled, null bean values will be serialized to the output.
3038       *
3039       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3040       *    <li class='note'>Not enabling this setting will cause <c>Map</c>s with <jk>null</jk> values to be lost during parsing.
3041       * </ul>
3042       *
3043       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3044       * <p class='bjava'>
3045       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that serializes null properties.</jc>
3046       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3047       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3048       *       .json()
3049       *       .keepNullProperties()
3050       *       .build();
3051       *
3052       *    <jc>// Our bean to serialize.</jc>
3053       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
3054       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <jk>null</jk>;
3055       *    }
3056       *
3057       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {foo:null}</jc>
3058       *    <jv>client</jv>
3059       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
3060       *       .run();
3061       * </p>
3062       *
3063       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3064       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#keepNullProperties()}
3065       * </ul>
3066       *
3067       * @return This object.
3068       */
3069      public SELF keepNullProperties() {
3070         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::keepNullProperties);
3071         return self();
3072      }

3074      /**
3075       * Logger.
3076       *
3077       * <p>
3078       * Specifies the logger to use for logging.
3079       *
3080       * <p>
3081       * If not specified, uses the following logger:
3082       * <p class='bjava'>
3083       *    Logger.<jsm>getLogger</jsm>(RestClient.<jk>class</jk>.getName());
3084       * </p>
3085       *
3086       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3087       * <p class='bjava'>
3088       *    <jc>// Construct a client that logs messages to a special logger.</jc>
3089       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3090       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3091       *       .logger(Logger.<jsm>getLogger</jsm>(<js>"MyLogger"</js>))  <jc>// Log to MyLogger logger.</jc>
3092       *       .logToConsole()  <jc>// Also log to console.</jc>
3093       *       .logRequests(<jsf>FULL</jsf>, <jsf>WARNING</jsf>)  <jc>// Log requests with full detail at WARNING level.</jc>
3094       *       .build();
3095       * </p>
3096       *
3097       * @param value The logger to use for logging.
3098       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (defaults to <c>Logger.getLogger(RestClient.class.getName())</c>).
3099       * @return This object.
3100       */
3101      public SELF logger(Logger value) {
3102         logger = value;
3103         return self();
3104      }
3105
3106      /**
3107       * Log requests.
3108       *
3109       * <p>
3110       * Causes requests/responses to be logged at the specified log level at the end of the request.
3111       *
3112       * <p>
3113       * <jsf>SIMPLE</jsf> detail produces a log message like the following:
3114       * <p class='bconsole'>
3115       *    POST http://localhost:10000/testUrl, HTTP/1.1 200 OK
3116       * </p>
3117       *
3118       * <p>
3119       * <jsf>FULL</jsf> detail produces a log message like the following:
3120       * <p class='bconsole'>
3121       *    === HTTP Call (outgoing) =======================================================
3122       *    === REQUEST ===
3123       *    POST http://localhost:10000/testUrl
3124       *    ---request headers---
3125       *       Debug: true
3126       *       No-Trace: true
3127       *       Accept: application/json
3128       *    ---request entity---
3129       *       Content-Type: application/json
3130       *    ---request content---
3131       *    {"foo":"bar","baz":123}
3132       *    === RESPONSE ===
3133       *    HTTP/1.1 200 OK
3134       *    ---response headers---
3135       *       Content-Type: application/json;charset=utf-8
3136       *       Content-Length: 21
3137       *       Server: Jetty(8.1.0.v20120127)
3138       *    ---response content---
3139       *    {"message":"OK then"}
3140       *    === END ========================================================================
3141       * </p>
3142       *
3143       * <p>
3144       * By default, the message is logged to the default logger.  It can be logged to a different logger via the
3145       * {@link #logger(Logger)} method or logged to the console using the
3146       * {@link #logToConsole()} method.
3147       *
3148       * @param detail The detail level of logging.
3149       * @param level The log level.
3150       * @param test A predicate to use per-request to see if the request should be logged.  If <jk>null</jk>, always logs.
3151       * @return This object.
3152       */
3153      public SELF logRequests(DetailLevel detail, Level level, BiPredicate<RestRequest,RestResponse> test) {
3154         logRequests = detail;
3155         logRequestsLevel = level;
3156         logRequestsPredicate = test;
3157         return self();
3158      }
3159
3160      /**
3161       * Log to console.
3162       *
3163       * <p>
3164       * Specifies to log messages to the console.
3165       *
3166       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3167       * <p class='bjava'>
3168       *    <jc>// Construct a client that logs messages to a special logger.</jc>
3169       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3170       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3171       *       .logToConsole()
3172       *       .logRequests(<jsf>FULL</jsf>, <jsf>INFO</jsf>)  <jc>// Level is ignored when logging to console.</jc>
3173       *       .build();
3174       * </p>
3175       *
3176       * @return This object.
3177       */
3178      public SELF logToConsole() {
3179         logToConsole = true;
3180         return self();
3181      }
3182
3183      /**
3184       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Marshaller
3185       *
3186       * <p>
3187       * Shortcut for specifying the serializers and parsers
3188       * using the serializer and parser defined in a marshaller.
3189       *
3190       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3191       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in a pre-instantiated serializers and parsers, the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}),
3192       *    parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}), or bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class have no effect.
3193       * </ul>
3194       *
3195       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3196       * <p class='bjava'>
3197       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses Simplified-JSON transport using an existing marshaller.</jc>
3198       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3199       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3200       *       .marshaller(Json5.<jsf>DEFAULT_READABLE</jsf>)
3201       *       .build();
3202       * </p>
3203       *
3204       * @param value The values to add to this setting.
3205       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (value will not be set, existing serializers and parsers will be kept).
3206       * @return This object.
3207       */
3208      public SELF marshaller(Marshaller value) {
3209         if (nn(value))
3210            serializer(value.getSerializer()).parser(value.getParser());
3211         return self();
3212      }
3213
3214      /**
3215       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Marshalls
3216       *
3217       * <p>
3218       * Shortcut for specifying the serializers and parsers
3219       * using the serializer and parser defined in a marshaller.
3220       *
3221       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3222       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in a pre-instantiated serializers and parsers, the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}),
3223       *    parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}), or bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class have no effect.
3224       * </ul>
3225       *
3226       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3227       * <p class='bjava'>
3228       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses JSON and XML transport using existing marshalls.</jc>
3229       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3230       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3231       *       .marshaller(Json.<jsf>DEFAULT_READABLE</jsf>, Xml.<jsf>DEFAULT_READABLE</jsf>)
3232       *       .build();
3233       * </p>
3234       *
3235       * @param value The values to add to this setting.
3236       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
3237       * @return This object.
3238       */
3239      public SELF marshallers(Marshaller...value) {
3240         for (var m : value)
3241            if (nn(m))
3242               serializer(m.getSerializer()).parser(m.getParser());
3243         return self();
3244      }
3245
3246      /**
3247       * Assigns maximum connection per route value.
3248       *
3249       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3250       *    <li class='note'>This value can be overridden by the {@link #connectionManager(HttpClientConnectionManager)} method.
3251       * </ul>
3252       *
3253       * @param maxConnPerRoute New property value.
3254       * @return This object.
3255       * @see HttpClientBuilder#setMaxConnPerRoute(int)
3256       */
3257      public SELF maxConnPerRoute(int maxConnPerRoute) {
3258         httpClientBuilder().setMaxConnPerRoute(maxConnPerRoute);
3259         return self();
3260      }
3261
3262      /**
3263       * Assigns maximum total connection value.
3264       *
3265       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3266       *    <li class='note'>This value can be overridden by the {@link #connectionManager(HttpClientConnectionManager)} method.
3267       * </ul>
3268       *
3269       * @param maxConnTotal New property value.
3270       * @return This object.
3271       * @see HttpClientBuilder#setMaxConnTotal(int)
3272       */
3273      public SELF maxConnTotal(int maxConnTotal) {
3274         httpClientBuilder().setMaxConnTotal(maxConnTotal);
3275         return self();
3276      }
3277
3278      /**
3279       * <i><l>BeanTraverse</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Max serialization depth.
3280       *
3281       * <p>
3282       * When enabled, abort traversal if specified depth is reached in the POJO tree.
3283       *
3284       * <p>
3285       * If this depth is exceeded, an exception is thrown.
3286       *
3287       * <p>
3288       * This prevents stack overflows from occurring when trying to traverse models with recursive references.
3289       *
3290       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3291       * <p class='bjava'>
3292       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that throws an exception if the depth reaches greater than 20.</jc>
3293       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3294       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3295       *       .json()
3296       *       .maxDepth(20)
3297       *       .build();
3298       * </p>
3299       *
3300       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3301       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext.Builder#maxDepth(int)}
3302       * </ul>
3303       *
3304       * @param value
3305       *    The new value for this property.
3306       *    <br>The default is <c>100</c>.
3307       * @return This object.
3308       */
3309      public SELF maxDepth(int value) {
3310         serializers().forEach(x -> x.maxDepth(value));
3311         return self();
3312      }
3313
3314      /**
3315       * <i><l>WriterSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Maximum indentation.
3316       *
3317       * <p>
3318       * Specifies the maximum indentation level in the serialized document.
3319       *
3320       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3321       *    <li class='note'>This setting does not apply to the RDF serializers.
3322       * </ul>
3323       *
3324       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3325       * <p class='bjava'>
3326       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that indents a maximum of 20 tabs.</jc>
3327       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3328       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3329       *       .json()
3330       *       .ws()  <jc>// Enable whitespace</jc>
3331       *       .maxIndent(20)
3332       *       .build();
3333       * </p>
3334       *
3335       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3336       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializer.Builder#maxIndent(int)}
3337       * </ul>
3338       *
3339       * @param value
3340       *    The new value for this property.
3341       *    <br>The default is <c>100</c>.
3342       * @return This object.
3343       */
3344      public SELF maxIndent(int value) {
3345         serializers().forEachWS(x -> x.maxIndent(value));
3346         return self();
3347      }
3348
3349      /**
3350       * Appends the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers on all requests made by this client.
3351       *
3352       * <p>
3353       * Headers are appended to the end of the current header list.
3354       *
3355       * <p>
3356       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(Accept.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>), ContentType.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>))</c>.
3357       *
3358       * @param value The new header values.
3359       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no Accept or Content-Type headers will be added).
3360       * @return This object.
3361       * @see #headers()
3362       */
3363      @Override
3364      public SELF mediaType(MediaType value) {
3365         super.mediaType(value);
3366         return headers(Accept.of(value), ContentType.of(value));
3367      }
3368
3369      /**
3370       * Appends the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers on all requests made by this client.
3371       *
3372       * <p>
3373       * Headers are appended to the end of the current header list.
3374       *
3375       * <p>
3376       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>headerData().append(Accept.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>), ContentType.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>value</jv>))</c>.
3377       *
3378       * @param value The new header values.
3379       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no Accept or Content-Type headers will be added).
3380       * @return This object.
3381       * @see #headers()
3382       */
3383      public SELF mediaType(String value) {
3384         super.mediaType(MediaType.of(value));
3385         return headers(Accept.of(value), ContentType.of(value));
3386      }
3387
3388      /**
3389       * Convenience method for specifying BSON as the marshalling transmission media type.
3390       *
3391       * <p>
3392       * BSON (Binary JSON) is the format used by MongoDB. Identical to calling
3393       * <c>serializer(BsonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(BsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
3394       *
3395       * @return This object.
3396       */
3397      public SELF bson() {
3398         return serializer(BsonSerializer.class).parser(BsonParser.class);
3399      }
3400
3401      /**
3402       * Convenience method for specifying MessagePack as the marshalling transmission media type.
3403       *
3404       * <p>
3405       * MessagePack is a binary equivalent to JSON that takes up considerably less space than JSON.
3406       *
3407       * <p>
3408       *    {@link MsgPackSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
3409       *    <ul>
3410       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
3411       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
3412       *    </ul>
3413       * <p>
3414       *    {@link MsgPackParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
3415       *    <ul>
3416       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
3417       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
3418       *    </ul>
3419       * <p>
3420       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/msgpack"</js> unless overridden
3421       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
3422       * <p>
3423       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/msgpack"</js> unless overridden
3424       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
3425       * <p>
3426       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
3427       *    <ul>
3428       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
3429       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
3430       *    </ul>
3431       * <p>
3432       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(MsgPackSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(MsgPackParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
3433       *
3434       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3435       * <p class='bjava'>
3436       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses MessagePack marshalling.</jc>
3437       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().msgPack().build();
3438       * </p>
3439       *
3440       * @return This object.
3441       */
3442      public SELF msgPack() {
3443         return serializer(MsgPackSerializer.class).parser(MsgPackParser.class);
3444      }
3445
3446      /**
3447       * When called, <c>No-Trace: true</c> is added to requests.
3448       *
3449       * <p>
3450       * This gives the opportunity for the servlet to not log errors on invalid requests.
3451       * This is useful for testing purposes when you don't want your log file to show lots of errors that are simply the
3452       * results of testing.
3453       *
3454       * <p>
3455       * It's up to the server to decide whether to allow for this.
3456       * The <c>BasicTestRestLogger</c> class watches for this header and prevents logging of status 400+ responses to
3457       * prevent needless logging of test scenarios.
3458       *
3459       * @return This object.
3460       * @see #headers()
3461       */
3462      public SELF noTrace() {
3463         return headers(NoTrace.of(true));
3464      }
3465
3466      /**
3467       * <i><l>OpenApiCommon</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Default collection format for HTTP parts.
3468       *
3469       * <p>
3470       * Specifies the collection format to use for HTTP parts when not otherwise specified via {@link Schema#collectionFormat()} for the
3471       * OpenAPI serializer and parser on this client.
3472       *
3473       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3474       * <p class='bjava'>
3475       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with CSV format for http parts.</jc>
3476       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3477       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3478       *       .collectionFormat(<jsf>CSV</jsf>)
3479       *       .build();
3480       *
3481       *    <jc>// An arbitrary data structure.</jc>
3482       *    AList <jv>list</jv> = AList.<jsm>of</jsm>(
3483       *       <js>"foo"</js>,
3484       *       <js>"bar"</js>,
3485       *       AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(
3486       *          <js>"baz"</js>, AList.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"qux"</js>,<js>"true"</js>,<js>"123"</js>)
3487       *    )
3488       * );
3489       *
3490       *    <jc>// Set a header with a comma-separated list.</jc>
3491       *    <jv>client</jv>
3492       *       .get(<js>"/uri"</js>)
3493       *       .header(<js>"Foo"</js>, <jv>list</jv>)  <jc>// Will be serialized as: foo=bar,baz=qux\,true\,123</jc>
3494       *       .run();
3495       * </p>
3496       *
3497       * <ul class='values javatree'>
3498       *    <li class='jc'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat}
3499       *    <ul>
3500       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#CSV CSV} - (default) Comma-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo,bar"</js>).
3501       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#SSV SSV} - Space-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo bar"</js>).
3502       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#TSV TSV} - Tab-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo\tbar"</js>).
3503       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#PIPES PIPES} - Pipe-separated values (e.g. <js>"foo|bar"</js>).
3504       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#MULTI MULTI} - Corresponds to multiple parameter instances instead of multiple values for a single instance (e.g. <js>"foo=bar&amp;foo=baz"</js>).
3505       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#UONC UONC} - UON collection notation (e.g. <js>"@(foo,bar)"</js>).
3506       *    </ul>
3507       * </ul>
3508       *
3509       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3510       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.OpenApiSerializer.Builder#collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat)}
3511       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.OpenApiParser.Builder#collectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat)}
3512       * </ul>
3513       *
3514       * @param value
3515       *    The new value for this property.
3516       *    <br>The default value is {@link HttpPartCollectionFormat#NO_COLLECTION_FORMAT}.
3517       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3518       * @return This object.
3519       */
3520      public SELF oapiCollectionFormat(HttpPartCollectionFormat value) {
3521         serializers().forEach(OpenApiSerializer.Builder.class, x -> x.collectionFormat(value));
3522         parsers().forEach(OpenApiParser.Builder.class, x -> x.collectionFormat(value));
3523         partSerializer().builder(OpenApiSerializer.Builder.class, x -> x.collectionFormat(value));
3524         partParser().builder(OpenApiParser.Builder.class, x -> x.collectionFormat(value));
3525         return self();
3526      }
3527
3528      /**
3529       * <i><l>OpenApiCommon</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Default OpenAPI format for HTTP parts.
3530       *
3531       * <p>
3532       * Specifies the format to use for HTTP parts when not otherwise specified via {@link Schema#format()} for
3533       * the OpenAPI serializer and parser on this client.
3534       *
3535       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3536       * <p class='bjava'>
3537       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with UON part serialization and parsing.</jc>
3538       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv>  = RestClient
3539       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3540       *       .oapiFormat(<jsf>UON</jsf>)
3541       *       .build();
3542       *
3543       *    <jc>// Set a header with a value in UON format.</jc>
3544       *    <jv>client</jv>
3545       *       .get(<js>"/uri"</js>)
3546       *       .header(<js>"Foo"</js>, <js>"bar baz"</js>)  <jc>// Will be serialized as:  'bar baz'</jc>
3547       *       .run();
3548       * </p>
3549       *
3550       * <ul class='values javatree'>
3551       *    <li class='jc'>{@link HttpPartFormat}
3552       *    <ul>
3553       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#UON UON} - UON notation (e.g. <js>"'foo bar'"</js>).
3554       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#INT32 INT32} - Signed 32 bits.
3555       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#INT64 INT64} - Signed 64 bits.
3556       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#FLOAT FLOAT} - 32-bit floating point number.
3557       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#DOUBLE DOUBLE} - 64-bit floating point number.
3558       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#BYTE BYTE} - BASE-64 encoded characters.
3559       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#BINARY BINARY} - Hexadecimal encoded octets (e.g. <js>"00FF"</js>).
3560       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#BINARY_SPACED BINARY_SPACED} - Spaced-separated hexadecimal encoded octets (e.g. <js>"00 FF"</js>).
3561       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#DATE DATE} - An <a href='http://xml2rfc.ietf.org/public/rfc/html/rfc3339.html#anchor14'>RFC3339 full-date</a>.
3562       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#DATE_TIME DATE_TIME} - An <a href='http://xml2rfc.ietf.org/public/rfc/html/rfc3339.html#anchor14'>RFC3339 date-time</a>.
3563       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#PASSWORD PASSWORD} - Used to hint UIs the input needs to be obscured.
3564       *       <li class='jf'>{@link HttpPartFormat#NO_FORMAT NO_FORMAT} - (default) Not specified.
3565       *    </ul>
3566       * </ul>
3567       *
3568       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3569       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.OpenApiSerializer.Builder#format(HttpPartFormat)}
3570       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.oapi.OpenApiParser.Builder#format(HttpPartFormat)}
3571       * </ul>
3572       *
3573       * @param value
3574       *    The new value for this property.
3575       *    <br>The default value is {@link HttpPartFormat#NO_FORMAT}.
3576       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3577       * @return This object.
3578       */
3579      public SELF oapiFormat(HttpPartFormat value) {
3580         serializers().forEach(OpenApiSerializer.Builder.class, x -> x.format(value));
3581         parsers().forEach(OpenApiParser.Builder.class, x -> x.format(value));
3582         partSerializer().builder(OpenApiSerializer.Builder.class).ifPresent(x -> x.format(value));
3583         partParser().builder(OpenApiParser.Builder.class).ifPresent(x -> x.format(value));
3584         return self();
3585      }
3586
3587      /**
3588       * Convenience method for specifying OpenAPI as the marshalling transmission media type.
3589       *
3590       * <p>
3591       * OpenAPI is a language that allows serialization to formats that use {@link HttpPartSchema} objects to describe their structure.
3592       *
3593       * <p>
3594       *    {@link OpenApiSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
3595       *    <ul>
3596       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
3597       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
3598       *       <li>Typically the {@link RestRequest#content(Object, HttpPartSchema)} method will be used to specify the body of the request with the
3599       *          schema describing it's structure.
3600       *    </ul>
3601       * <p>
3602       *    {@link OpenApiParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
3603       *    <ul>
3604       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
3605       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
3606       *       <li>Typically the {@link ResponseContent#schema(HttpPartSchema)} method will be used to specify the structure of the response body.
3607       *    </ul>
3608       * <p>
3609       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/openapi"</js> unless overridden
3610       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
3611       * <p>
3612       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/openapi"</js> unless overridden
3613       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
3614       * <p>
3615       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
3616       *    <ul>
3617       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
3618       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
3619       *    </ul>
3620       * <p>
3621       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(OpenApiSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(OpenApiParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
3622       *
3623       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3624       * <p class='bjava'>
3625       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses OpenAPI marshalling.</jc>
3626       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().openApi().build();
3627       * </p>
3628       *
3629       * @return This object.
3630       */
3631      public SELF openApi() {
3632         return serializer(OpenApiSerializer.class).parser(OpenApiParser.class);
3633      }
3634
3635      /**
3636       * <i><l>UonSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Parameter format.
3637       *
3638       * <p>
3639       * Specifies the format of parameters when using the {@link UrlEncodingSerializer} to serialize Form Posts.
3640       *
3641       * <p>
3642       * Specifies the format to use for GET parameter keys and values.
3643       *
3644       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3645       * <p class='bjava'>
3646       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with URL-Encoded serializer that serializes values in plain-text format.</jc>
3647       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3648       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3649       *       .urlEnc()
3650       *       .paramFormat(<jsf>PLAINTEXT</jsf>)
3651       *       .build();
3652       *
3653       *    <jc>// An arbitrary data structure.</jc>
3654       *    AMap <jv>map</jv> = AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(
3655       *       <js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>,
3656       *       <js>"baz"</js>, <jk>new</jk> String[]{<js>"qux"</js>, <js>"true"</js>, <js>"123"</js>}
3657       *    );
3658       *
3659       *    <jc>// Request body will be serialized as:  foo=bar,baz=qux,true,123</jc>
3660       *    <jv>client</jv>
3661       *       .post(<js>"/uri"</js>, <jv>map</jv>)
3662       *       .run();
3663       * </p>
3664       *
3665       * <ul class='values javatree'>
3666       *    <li class='jf'>{@link ParamFormat#UON} (default) - Use UON notation for parameters.
3667       *    <li class='jf'>{@link ParamFormat#PLAINTEXT} - Use plain text for parameters.
3668       * </ul>
3669       *
3670       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3671       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.uon.UonSerializer.Builder#paramFormat(ParamFormat)}
3672       * </ul>
3673       *
3674       * @param value The new value for this property.
3675       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3676       * @return This object.
3677       */
3678      public SELF paramFormat(ParamFormat value) {
3679         serializers().forEach(UonSerializer.Builder.class, x -> x.paramFormat(value));
3680         return self();
3681      }
3682
3683      /**
3684       * <i><l>UonSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Parameter format.
3685       *
3686       * <p>
3687       * Specifies the format of parameters when using the {@link UrlEncodingSerializer} to serialize Form Posts.
3688       *
3689       * <p>
3690       * Specifies plaintext as the format to use for GET parameter keys and values.
3691       *
3692       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3693       * <p class='bjava'>
3694       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with URL-Encoded serializer that serializes values in plain-text format.</jc>
3695       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3696       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3697       *       .urlEnc()
3698       *       .build();
3699       *
3700       *    <jc>// An arbitrary data structure.</jc>
3701       *    AMap <jv>map</jv> = AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(
3702       *       <js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>,
3703       *       <js>"baz"</js>, <jk>new</jk> String[]{<js>"qux"</js>, <js>"true"</js>, <js>"123"</js>}
3704       *    );
3705       *
3706       *    <jc>// Request body will be serialized as:  foo=bar,baz=qux,true,123</jc>
3707       *    <jv>client</jv>
3708       *       .post(<js>"/uri"</js>, <jv>map</jv>)
3709       *       .run();
3710       * </p>
3711       *
3712       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
3713       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.uon.UonSerializer.Builder#paramFormatPlain()}
3714       * </ul>
3715       *
3716       * @return This object.
3717       */
3718      public SELF paramFormatPlain() {
3719         serializers().forEach(UonSerializer.Builder.class, UonSerializer.Builder::paramFormatPlain);
3720         return self();
3721      }

3723      /**
3724       * Parser.
3725       *
3726       * <p>
3727       * Associates the specified {@link Parser Parser} with the HTTP client.
3728       *
3729       * <p>
3730       * The parser is used to parse the HTTP response body into a POJO.
3731       *
3732       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3733       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in a class, the parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
3734       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
3735       * </ul>
3736       *
3737       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3738       * <p class='bjava'>
3739       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses JSON transport for response bodies.</jc>
3740       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3741       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3742       *       .parser(JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>)
3743       *       .build();
3744       * </p>
3745       *
3746       * @param value
3747       *    The new value for this setting.
3748       *    <br>The default value is {@link JsonParser#DEFAULT}.
3749       * @return This object.
3750       */
3751      @SuppressWarnings({
3752         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
3753      })
3754      public SELF parser(Class<? extends Parser> value) {
3755         return parsers(value);
3756      }

3758      /**
3759       * Parser.
3760       *
3761       * <p>
3762       * Associates the specified {@link Parser Parser} with the HTTP client.
3763       *
3764       * <p>
3765       * The parser is used to parse the HTTP response body into a POJO.
3766       *
3767       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3768       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in a pre-instantiated parser, the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
3769       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined
3770       *    on this builder class have no effect.
3771       * </ul>
3772       *
3773       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3774       * <p class='bjava'>
3775       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses a predefined JSON parser for response bodies.</jc>
3776       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3777       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3778       *       .parser(JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>)
3779       *       .build();
3780       * </p>

3782       * @param value
3783       *    The new value for this setting.
3784       *    <br>The default value is {@link JsonParser#DEFAULT}.

3786       * @return This object.
3787       */
3788      public SELF parser(Parser value) {
3789         return parsers(value);

3803      /**
3804       * Parsers.
3805       *
3806       * <p>
3807       * Associates the specified {@link Parser Parsers} with the HTTP client.
3808       *
3809       * <p>
3810       * The parsers are used to parse the HTTP response body into a POJO.
3811       *
3812       * <p>
3813       * The parser that best matches the <c>Accept</c> header will be used to parse the response body.
3814       * <br>If no <c>Accept</c> header is specified, the first parser in the list will be used.
3815       *
3816       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3817       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in classes, the parsers can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
3818       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
3819       * </ul>
3820       *
3821       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3822       * <p class='bjava'>
3823       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses JSON and XML transport for response bodies.</jc>
3824       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3825       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3826       *       .parser(JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)
3827       *       .build();
3828       * </p>
3829       *
3830       * @param value
3831       *    The new value for this setting.
3832       *    <br>The default value is {@link JsonParser#DEFAULT}.
3833       * @return This object.
3834       */
3835      @SuppressWarnings({
3836         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
3837      })
3838      public SELF parsers(Class<? extends Parser>...value) {
3839         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_value, value);
3840         parsers().add(value);
3841         return self();
3842      }
3843
3844      /**
3845       * Parsers.
3846       *
3847       * <p>
3848       * Associates the specified {@link Parser Parsers} with the HTTP client.
3849       *
3850       * <p>
3851       * The parsers are used to parse the HTTP response body into a POJO.
3852       *
3853       * <p>
3854       * The parser that best matches the <c>Accept</c> header will be used to parse the response body.
3855       * <br>If no <c>Accept</c> header is specified, the first parser in the list will be used.
3856       *
3857       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
3858       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in pre-instantiated parsers, the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
3859       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined
3860       *    on this builder class have no effect.
3861       * </ul>
3862       *
3863       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
3864       * <p class='bjava'>
3865       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses JSON and XML transport for response bodies.</jc>
3866       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
3867       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
3868       *       .parser(JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>, XmlParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>)
3869       *       .build();
3870       * </p>
3871       *
3872       * @param value
3873       *    The new value for this setting.
3874       *    <br>The default value is {@link JsonParser#DEFAULT}.
3875       * @return This object.
3876       */
3877      public SELF parsers(Parser...value) {
3878         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_value, value);
3879         parsers().add(value);
3880         return self();
3881      }

3915       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
3916       * @return This object.

3918      public SELF partParser(Class<? extends HttpPartParser> value) {
3919         partParser().type(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
3920         return self();
