4021      /**
4022       * Returns the builder for the list of path data parameters that get applied to all requests created by this builder.
4023       *
4024       * <p>
4025       * This is the primary method for accessing the path data parameter list.
4026       * On first call, the builder is created via the method {@link #createFormData()}.
4027       *
4028       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4029       * <p class='bjava'>
4030       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses "bar" for the "{foo}" path variable on every request.</jc>
4031       *    RestClient.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>();
4032       *    <jv>builder</jv>.pathData().setDefault(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>));
4033       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jv>builder</jv>.build();
4034       * </p>
4035       *
4036       * <p>
4037       * The following convenience methods are also provided for updating the parameters:
4038       * <ul>
4039       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #pathData(NameValuePair...)}
4040       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #pathDataDefault(NameValuePair...)}
4041       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #pathData(String,String)}
4042       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #pathData(String,Supplier)}
4043       * </ul>
4044       *
4045       * @return The form data list builder.
4046       */
4047      public final PartList pathData() {
4048         if (pathData == null)
4049            pathData = createPathData();
4050         return pathData;
4051      }
4052
4053      /**
4054       * Sets multiple path parameters on all requests.
4055       *
4056       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4057       * <p class='bjava'>
4058       *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.http.HttpParts.*;
4059       *
4060       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4061       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4062       *       .pathData(
4063       *          <jsm>stringPart</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
4064       *          <jsm>booleanPart</jsm>(<js>"baz"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
4065       *       )
4066       *       .build();
4067       * </p>
4068       *
4069       * <p>
4070       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>pathData().append(<jv>parts</jv>)</c>.
4071       *
4072       * @param parts
4073       *    The path parameters.
4074       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
4075       * @return This object.
4076       * @see #pathData()
4077       */
4078      public SELF pathData(NameValuePair...parts) {
4079         pathData().append(parts);
4080         return self();
4081      }
4082
4083      /**
4084       * Appends a path parameter to all request bodies.
4085       *
4086       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4087       * <p class='bjava'>
4088       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4089       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4090       *       .pathData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
4091       *       .build();
4092       * </p>
4093       *
4094       * <p>
4095       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>pathData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
4096       *
4097       * @param name The parameter name.
4098       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
4099       * @param value The parameter value.
4100       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (null value will be serialized).
4101       * @return This object.
4102       * @see #pathData()
4103       */
4104      public SELF pathData(String name, String value) {
4105         pathData().append(name, value);
4106         return self();
4107      }
4108
4109      /**
4110       * Sets a path parameter with a dynamic value to all request bodies.
4111       *
4112       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4113       * <p class='bjava'>
4114       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4115       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4116       *       .pathData(<js>"foo"</js>, ()-&gt;<js>"bar"</js>)
4117       *       .build();
4118       * </p>
4119       *
4120       * <p>
4121       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>pathData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
4122       *
4123       * @param name The parameter name.
4124       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
4125       * @param value The parameter value supplier.
4126       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (null value will be serialized).
4127       * @return This object.
4128       * @see #pathData()
4129       */
4130      public SELF pathData(String name, Supplier<String> value) {
4131         pathData().set(name, value);
4132         return self();
4133      }

4135      /**
4136       * Sets default path parameter values.
4137       *
4138       * <p>
4139       * Uses default values for specified parameters if not otherwise specified on the outgoing requests.
4140       *
4141       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4142       * <p class='bjava'>
4143       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4144       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4145       *       .pathDataDefault(<jsm>stringPart</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, ()-&gt;<js>"bar"</js>));
4146       *       .build();
4147       * </p>
4148       *
4149       * <p>
4150       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>pathData().setDefault(<jv>parts</jv>)</c>.
4151       *
4152       * @param parts The parts.
4153       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
4154       * @return This object.
4155       * @see #pathData()
4156       */
4157      public SELF pathDataDefault(NameValuePair...parts) {
4158         pathData().setDefault(parts);
4159         return self();
4160      }

4162      /**
4163       * Convenience method for specifying Plain Text as the marshalling transmission media type.
4164       *
4165       * <p>
4166       * Plain text marshalling typically only works on simple POJOs that can be converted to and from strings using
4167       * swaps, swap methods, etc...
4168       *
4169       * <p>
4170       *    {@link PlainTextSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
4171       *    <ul>
4172       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
4173       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
4174       *    </ul>
4175       * <p>
4176       *    {@link PlainTextParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
4177       *    <ul>
4178       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
4179       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
4180       *    </ul>
4181       * <p>
4182       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/plain"</js> unless overridden
4183       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
4184       * <p>
4185       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/plain"</js> unless overridden
4186       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
4187       * <p>
4188       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
4189       *    <ul>
4190       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
4191       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
4192       *    </ul>
4193       * <p>
4194       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(PlainTextSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(PlainTextParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
4195       *
4196       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4197       * <p class='bjava'>
4198       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses Plain Text marshalling.</jc>
4199       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().plainText().build();
4200       * </p>
4201       *
4202       * @return This object.
4203       */
4204      public SELF plainText() {
4205         return serializer(PlainTextSerializer.class).parser(PlainTextParser.class);
4206      }

4275      /**
4276       * Returns the builder for the list of query parameters that get applied to all requests created by this builder.
4277       *
4278       * <p>
4279       * This is the primary method for accessing the query parameter list.
4280       * On first call, the builder is created via the method {@link #createQueryData()}.
4281       *
4282       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4283       * <p class='bjava'>
4284       *    <jc>// Create a client that adds a "foo=bar" query parameter on every request.</jc>
4285       *    RestClient.Builder<?> <jv>builder</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>();
4286       *    <jv>builder</jv>.queryData().setDefault(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>));
4287       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jv>builder</jv>.build();
4288       * </p>
4289       *
4290       * <p>
4291       * The following convenience methods are also provided for updating the parameters:
4292       * <ul>
4293       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #queryData(NameValuePair...)}
4294       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #queryDataDefault(NameValuePair...)}
4295       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #queryData(String,String)}
4296       *    <li class='jm'>{@link #queryData(String,Supplier)}
4297       * </ul>
4298       *
4299       * @return The query data list builder.
4300       */
4301      public final PartList queryData() {
4302         if (queryData == null)
4303            queryData = createQueryData();
4304         return queryData;
4305      }
4306
4307      /**
4308       * Appends multiple query parameters to the URI of all requests.
4309       *
4310       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4311       * <p class='bjava'>
4312       *    <jk>import static</jk> org.apache.juneau.http.HttpParts.*;
4313       *
4314       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4315       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4316       *       .queryData(
4317       *          <jsm>stringPart</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>),
4318       *          <jsm>booleanPart</jsm>(<js>"baz"</js>, <jk>true</jk>)
4319       *       )
4320       *       .build();
4321       * </p>
4322       *
4323       * <p>
4324       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>queryData().append(<jv>parts</jv>)</c>.
4325       *
4326       * @param parts
4327       *    The query parameters.
4328       *    <br>Can contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
4329       * @return This object.
4330       * @see #queryData()
4331       */
4332      public SELF queryData(NameValuePair...parts) {
4333         queryData().append(parts);
4334         return self();
4335      }

4337      /**
4338       * Appends a query parameter to the URI.
4339       *
4340       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4341       * <p class='bjava'>
4342       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4343       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4344       *       .queryData(<js>"foo"</js>, <js>"bar"</js>)
4345       *       .build();
4346       * </p>
4347       *
4348       * <p>
4349       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>queryData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
4350       *
4351       * @param name The parameter name.
4352       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
4353       * @param value The parameter value.
4354       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (null value will be serialized).
4355       * @return This object.
4356       * @see #queryData()
4357       */
4358      public SELF queryData(String name, String value) {
4359         queryData().append(name, value);
4360         return self();
4361      }

4363      /**
4364       * Appends a query parameter with a dynamic value to the URI.

4368       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4369       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4370       *       .queryData(<js>"foo"</js>, ()-&gt;<js>"bar"</js>)
4371       *       .build();
4372       * </p>
4373       *
4374       * <p>
4375       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>queryData().append(<jv>name</jv>,<jv>value</jv>)</c>.
4376       *
4377       * @param name The parameter name.
4378       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
4379       * @param value The parameter value supplier.
4380       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (null value will be serialized).
4381       * @return This object.
4382       * @see #queryData()
4383       */
4384      public SELF queryData(String name, Supplier<String> value) {
4385         queryData().append(name, value);
4386         return self();
4387      }

4450      /**
4451       * Convenience for {@link #serializerSessionOptionsQueryDefault(String)} using {@link Uon#of(Object)}.
4452       *

4454       *    Property names and values for serializer session options.
4455       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as an empty map).
4456       * @return This object.
4457       */
4458      public SELF serializerSessionOptionsQueryDefault(Map<String,?> properties) {
4459         try {
4460            return serializerSessionOptionsQueryDefault(isEmpty(properties) ? null : Uon.of(properties));
4461         } catch (SerializeException e) {
4462            throw rex(e, "Could not serialize serializer session options query default");
4463         }
4464      }

4482      /**
4483       * <i><l>WriterSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Quote character.
4484       *
4485       * <p>
4486       * Specifies the character to use for quoting attributes and values.
4487       *
4488       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
4489       *    <li class='note'>This setting does not apply to the RDF serializers.
4490       * </ul>
4491       *
4492       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4493       * <p class='bjava'>
4494       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that uses single quotes.</jc>
4495       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4496       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4497       *       .json()
4498       *       .quoteChar(<js>'\''</js>)
4499       *       .build();
4500       *
4501       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property</jc>
4502       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
4503       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
4504       *    }
4505       *
4506       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {'foo':'bar'}</jc>
4507       *    <jv>client</jv>
4508       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
4509       *       .run();
4510       * </p>
4511       *
4512       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
4513       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializer.Builder#quoteChar(char)}
4514       * </ul>
4515       *
4516       * @param value
4517       *    The new value for this property.
4518       *    <br>The default is <js>'"'</js>.
4519       * @return This object.
4520       */
4521      public SELF quoteChar(char value) {
4522         serializers().forEachWS(x -> x.quoteChar(value));
4523         return self();
4524      }

4573      /**
4574       * <i><l>RestClient</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Root URI.
4575       *
4576       * <p>
4577       * When set, relative URI strings passed in through the various rest call methods (e.g. {@link RestClient#get(Object)}
4578       * will be prefixed with the specified root.
4579       * <br>This root URI is ignored on those methods if you pass in a {@link URL}, {@link URI}, or an absolute URI string.
4580       *
4581       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4582       * <p class='bjava'>
4583       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses UON format by default for HTTP parts.</jc>
4584       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4585       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4586       *       .rootUrl(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>)
4587       *       .build();
4588       *
4589       *    Bar <jv>bar</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
4590       *       .get(<js>"/bar"</js>)  <jc>// Relative to http://localhost:10000/foo</jc>
4591       *       .run()
4592       *       .getContent().as(Bar.<jk>class</jk>);
4593       * </p>
4594       *
4595       * @param value
4596       *    The root URI to prefix to relative URI strings.
4597       *    <br>Trailing slashes are trimmed.
4598       *    <br>Usually a <c>String</c> but you can also pass in <c>URI</c> and <c>URL</c> objects as well.
4599       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no root URL will be set).
4600       * @return This object.
4601       */
4602      public SELF rootUrl(Object value) {
4603         var s = s(value);
4604         if (ine(s))
4605            s = s.replaceAll("\\/$", "");
4606         if (isEmpty(s))
4607            rootUrl = null;
4608         else if (s.indexOf("://") == -1)
4609            throw rex("Invalid rootUrl value: ''{0}''.  Must be a valid absolute URL.", value);
4610         else {
4611            final var url = s;
4612            rootUrl = () -> url;
4613         }
4614         return self();
4615      }
4616
4617      /**
4618       * Sets the root URL using a supplier, allowing the URL to be computed dynamically at request time.
4619       *
4620       * <p>
4621       * The supplier is called on every request, so the URL can change between requests.
4622       * No build-time validation is performed; invalid values will surface as errors when requests are made.
4623       *
4624       * <p>
4625       * Three common patterns are supported:
4626       * <ul>
4627       *    <li>
4628       *       <b>Swappable reference</b> &mdash; Use an {@link AtomicReference AtomicReference}
4629       *       when you need to point the client at a different host at any time:
4630       *       <p class='bjava'>
4631       *          AtomicReference&lt;String&gt; <jv>urlRef</jv> = <jk>new</jk> AtomicReference&lt;&gt;(<js>"https://host1"</js>);
4632       *          RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().rootUrl(<jv>urlRef</jv>::get).build();
4633       *
4634       *          <jc>// Switch to a different host at runtime.</jc>
4635       *          <jv>urlRef</jv>.set(<js>"https://host2"</js>);
4636       *       </p>
4637       *    <li>
4638       *       <b>Refreshable cached value</b> &mdash; Use {@link Memoizer Memoizer}
4639       *       (via {@link Suppliers#memoizer(Supplier) Suppliers.memoizer()})
4640       *       when computing the URL is expensive (e.g. service discovery) and you want it cached until explicitly refreshed:
4641       *       <p class='bjava'>
4642       *          Memoizer&lt;String&gt; <jv>url</jv> = <jsm>memoizer</jsm>(() -&gt; serviceDiscovery.<jsm>findUrl</jsm>(<js>"my-service"</js>));
4643       *          RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().rootUrl(<jv>url</jv>).build();
4644       *
4645       *          <jc>// Force re-evaluation on the next request (e.g. after a failover).</jc>
4646       *          <jv>url</jv>.reset();
4647       *       </p>
4648       *    <li>
4649       *       <b>Purely dynamic</b> &mdash; Use a plain lambda when the URL must be re-evaluated on every request:
4650       *       <p class='bjava'>
4651       *          RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().rootUrl(() -&gt; config.<jsm>getRootUrl</jsm>()).build();
4652       *       </p>
4653       * </ul>
4654       *
4655       * @param value
4656       *    A supplier that returns the root URI to prefix to relative URI strings.
4657       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (no root URL will be set).
4658       * @return This object.
4659       */
4660      public SELF rootUrl(Supplier<String> value) {
4661         rootUrl = value;
4662         return self();
4663      }
4664

4670       * @return This object.
4671       * @see HttpClientBuilder#setRoutePlanner(HttpRoutePlanner)
4672       */
4673      public SELF routePlanner(HttpRoutePlanner routePlanner) {
4674         httpClientBuilder().setRoutePlanner(routePlanner);

4691      /**
4692       * Serializer.
4693       *
4694       * <p>
4695       * Associates the specified {@link Serializer Serializer} with the HTTP client.
4696       *
4697       * <p>
4698       * The serializer is used to serialize POJOs into the HTTP request body.
4699       *
4700       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
4701       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in a class, the serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
4702       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
4703       * </ul>
4704       *
4705       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4706       * <p class='bjava'>
4707       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses JSON transport for request bodies.</jc>
4708       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4709       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4710       *       .serializer(JsonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>)
4711       *       .sortCollections()  <jc>// Sort any collections being serialized.</jc>
4712       *       .build();
4713       * </p>
4714       *
4715       * @param value
4716       *    The new value for this setting.
4717       *    <br>The default is {@link JsonSerializer}.
4718       * @return This object.
4719       */
4720      @SuppressWarnings({
4721         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
4722      })
4723      public SELF serializer(Class<? extends Serializer> value) {
4724         return serializers(value);
4725      }

4727      /**
4728       * Serializer.
4729       *
4730       * <p>
4731       * Associates the specified {@link Serializer Serializer} with the HTTP client.
4732       *
4733       * <p>
4734       * The serializer is used to serialize POJOs into the HTTP request body.
4735       *
4736       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
4737       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in a pre-instantiated serializer, the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
4738       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined
4739       *    on this builder class have no effect.
4740       * </ul>
4741       *
4742       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4743       * <p class='bjava'>
4744       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses a predefined JSON serializer request bodies.</jc>
4745       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4746       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4747       *       .serializer(JsonSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT_READABLE</jsf>)
4748       *       .build();
4749       * </p>
4750       *
4751       * @param value
4752       *    The new value for this setting.
4753       *    <br>The default is {@link JsonSerializer}.
4754       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
4755       * @return This object.
4756       */
4757      public SELF serializer(Serializer value) {
4758         return serializers(value);
4759      }
4760
4761      /**
4762       * Returns the serializer group sub-builder.
4763       *
4764       * @return The serializer group sub-builder.
4765       */
4766      public final SerializerSet.Builder serializers() {
4767         if (serializers == null)
4768            serializers = createSerializers();
4769         return serializers;
4770      }

4772      /**
4773       * Serializers.
4774       *
4775       * <p>
4776       * Associates the specified {@link Serializer Serializers} with the HTTP client.
4777       *
4778       * <p>
4779       * The serializer is used to serialize POJOs into the HTTP request body.
4780       *
4781       * <p>
4782       * The serializer that best matches the <c>Content-Type</c> header will be used to serialize the request body.
4783       * <br>If no <c>Content-Type</c> header is specified, the first serializer in the list will be used.
4784       *
4785       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
4786       *    <li class='note'>When using this method that takes in classes, the serializers can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
4787       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
4788       * </ul>
4789       *
4790       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4791       * <p class='bjava'>
4792       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses JSON and XML transport for request bodies.</jc>
4793       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4794       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4795       *       .serializers(JsonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>, XmlSerializer.<jk>class</jk>)
4796       *       .sortCollections()  <jc>// Sort any collections being serialized.</jc>
4797       *       .build();
4798       * </p>
4799       *
4800       * @param value
4801       *    The new value for this setting.
4802       *    <br>The default is {@link JsonSerializer}.
4803       * @return This object.
4804       */
4805      @SuppressWarnings({
4806         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
4807      })
4808      public SELF serializers(Class<? extends Serializer>...value) {
4809         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_value, value);
4810         serializers().add(value);
4811         return self();
4812      }
4813
4814      /**

4817       * <p>
4818       * Associates the specified {@link Serializer Serializers} with the HTTP client.
4819       *
4820       * <p>
4821       * The serializer is used to serialize POJOs into the HTTP request body.
4822       *
4823       * <p>
4824       * The serializer that best matches the <c>Content-Type</c> header will be used to serialize the request body.
4825       * <br>If no <c>Content-Type</c> header is specified, the first serializer in the list will be used.
4826       *
4827       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>

4829       *    bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined
4830       *    on this builder class have no effect.
4831       * </ul>
4832       *
4833       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>

4835       *    <jc>// Create a client that uses predefined JSON and XML serializers for request bodies.</jc>
4836       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4837       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4838       *       .serializers(JsonSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT_READABLE</jsf>, XmlSerializer.<jsf>DEFAULT_READABLE</jsf>)
4839       *       .build();

4841       *
4842       * @param value
4843       *    The new value for this setting.
4844       *    <br>The default is {@link JsonSerializer}.
4845       * @return This object.
4846       */
4847      public SELF serializers(Serializer...value) {
4848         assertArgNoNulls(ARG_value, value);
4849         serializers().add(value);
4850         return self();
4851      }

4974      /**
4975       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Sort arrays and collections alphabetically.
4976       *
4977       * <p>
4978       * When enabled, copies and sorts the contents of arrays and collections before serializing them.
4979       *
4980       * <p>
4981       * Note that this introduces a performance penalty since it requires copying the existing collection.
4982       *
4983       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
4984       * <p class='bjava'>
4985       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that sorts arrays and collections before serialization.</jc>
4986       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
4987       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
4988       *       .json()
4989       *       .sortCollections()
4990       *       .build();
4991       *
4992       *    <jc>// An unsorted array</jc>
4993       *    String[] <jv>array</jv> = {<js>"foo"</js>,<js>"bar"</js>,<js>"baz"</js>}
4994       *
4995       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  ["bar","baz","foo"]</jc>
4996       *    <jv>client</jv>
4997       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>array</jv>)
4998       *       .run();
4999       * </p>
5000       *
5001       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5002       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#sortCollections()}
5003       * </ul>
5004       *
5005       * @return This object.
5006       */
5007      public SELF sortCollections() {
5008         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::sortCollections);
5009         return self();
5010      }
5011
5012      /**
5013       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Sort maps alphabetically.
5014       *
5015       * <p>
5016       * When enabled, copies and sorts the contents of maps by their keys before serializing them.
5017       *
5018       * <p>
5019       * Note that this introduces a performance penalty.
5020       *
5021       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5022       * <p class='bjava'>
5023       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that sorts maps before serialization.</jc>
5024       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
5025       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5026       *       .json()
5027       *       .sortMaps()
5028       *       .build();
5029       *
5030       *    <jc>// An unsorted map.</jc>
5031       *    AMap <jv>map</jv> = AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>,1,<js>"bar"</js>,2,<js>"baz"</js>,3);
5032       *
5033       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {"bar":2,"baz":3,"foo":1}</jc>
5034       *    <jv>client</jv>
5035       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>map</jv>)
5036       *       .run();
5037       * </p>
5038       *
5039       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5040       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#sortMaps()}
5041       * </ul>
5042       *
5043       * @return This object.
5044       */
5045      public SELF sortMaps() {
5046         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::sortMaps);
5047         return self();
5048      }
5049
5050      /**
5051       * <i><l>WriterSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Quote character.
5052       *
5053       * <p>
5054       * Specifies to use single quotes for quoting attributes and values.
5055       *
5056       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
5057       *    <li class='note'>This setting does not apply to the RDF serializers.
5058       * </ul>
5059       *
5060       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5061       * <p class='bjava'>
5062       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that uses single quotes.</jc>
5063       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
5064       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5065       *       .json()
5066       *       .sq()
5067       *       .build();
5068       *
5069       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property</jc>
5070       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
5071       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
5072       *    }
5073       *
5074       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {'foo':'bar'}</jc>
5075       *    <jv>client</jv>
5076       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
5077       *       .run();
5078       * </p>
5079       *
5080       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5081       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializer.Builder#quoteChar(char)}
5082       * </ul>
5083       *
5084       * @return This object.
5085       */
5086      public SELF sq() {
5087         serializers().forEachWS(WriterSerializer.Builder::sq);
5088         return self();
5089      }

5157      /**
5158       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Trim empty lists and arrays.
5159       *
5160       * <p>
5161       * When enabled, empty lists and arrays will not be serialized.
5162       *
5163       * <p>
5164       * Note that enabling this setting has the following effects on parsing:
5165       * <ul class='spaced-list'>
5166       *    <li>
5167       *       Map entries with empty list values will be lost.
5168       *    <li>
5169       *       Bean properties with empty list values will not be set.
5170       * </ul>
5171       *
5172       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5173       * <p class='bjava'>
5174       *    <jc>// Create a serializer that skips empty arrays and collections.</jc>
5175       *    WriterSerializer <jv>serializer</jv> = JsonSerializer
5176       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5177       *       .trimEmptyCollections()
5178       *       .build();
5179       *
5180       *    <jc>// A bean with a field with an empty array.</jc>
5181       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
5182       *       <jk>public</jk> String[] <jf>foo</jf> = {};
5183       *    }
5184       *
5185       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {}</jc>
5186       *    <jv>client</jv>
5187       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
5188       *       .run();
5189       * </p>
5190       *
5191       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5192       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#trimEmptyCollections()}
5193       * </ul>
5194       *
5195       * @return This object.
5196       */
5197      public SELF trimEmptyCollections() {
5198         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::trimEmptyCollections);
5199         return self();
5200      }
5201

5220       *       .json()
5221       *       .trimEmptyMaps()

5228       *
5229       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {}</jc>
5230       *    <jv>client</jv>
5231       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
5232       *       .run();
5233       * </p>
5234       *
5235       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5236       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#trimEmptyMaps()}
5237       * </ul>
5238       *

5246      /**
5247       * <i><l>Parser</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Trim parsed strings.
5248       *
5249       * <p>
5250       * When enabled, string values will be trimmed of whitespace using {@link String#trim()} before being added to
5251       * the POJO.
5252       *
5253       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5254       * <p class='bjava'>
5255       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON parser with trim-strings enabled.</jc>
5256       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
5257       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5258       *       .json()
5259       *       .trimStringsOnRead()
5260       *       .build();
5261       *
5262       *    <jc>// Try to parse JSON containing {" foo ":" bar "}.</jc>
5263       *    Map&lt;String,String&gt; <jv>map</jv> = <jv>client</jv>
5264       *       .get(<js>"/pathToJson"</js>)
5265       *       .run()
5266       *       .getContent().as(HashMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
5267       *
5268       *    <jc>// Make sure strings are trimmed.</jc>
5269       *    <jsm>assertEquals</jsm>(<js>"bar"</js>, <jv>map</jv>.get(<js>"foo"</js>));
5270       * </p>
5271       *
5272       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5273       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.Parser.Builder#trimStrings()}
5274       * </ul>
5275       *
5276       * @return This object.
5277       */
5278      public SELF trimStringsOnRead() {
5279         parsers().forEach(Parser.Builder::trimStrings);
5280         return self();
5281      }
5282
5283      /**
5284       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Trim strings.
5285       *
5286       * <p>
5287       * When enabled, string values will be trimmed of whitespace using {@link String#trim()} before being serialized.
5288       *
5289       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5290       * <p class='bjava'>
5291       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer that trims strings before serialization.</jc>
5292       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
5293       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5294       *       .json()
5295       *       .trimStrings()
5296       *       .build();
5297       *
5298       * <jc>// A map with space-padded keys/values</jc>
5299       *    AMap <jv>map</jv> = AMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>" foo "</js>, <js>" bar "</js>);
5300       *
5301       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {"foo":"bar"}</jc>
5302       *    <jv>client</jv>
5303       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>map</jv>)
5304       *       .run();
5305       * </p>
5306       *
5307       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5308       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#trimStrings()}
5309       * </ul>
5310       *
5311       * @return This object.
5312       */
5313      public SELF trimStringsOnWrite() {
5314         serializers().forEach(Serializer.Builder::trimStrings);
5315         return self();
5316      }

5318      /**
5319       * Convenience method for specifying all available transmission types.
5320       *
5321       * <p>
5322       *    All basic Juneau serializers will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
5323       *    <ul>
5324       *       <li>The serializers can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
5325       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5326       *    </ul>
5327       * <p>
5328       *    All basic Juneau parsers will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
5329       *    <ul>
5330       *       <li>The parsers can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
5331       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5332       *    </ul>
5333       * <p>
5334       *    <c>Accept</c> request header must be set via {@link #headers()}, or per-request
5335       *       via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)} in order for the correct parser to be selected.
5336       * <p>
5337       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header must be set via {@link #headers()},
5338       *       or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)} in order for the correct serializer to be selected.
5339       * <p>
5340       *    Similar to calling <c>json().json5().html().xml().uon().urlEnc().openApi().msgPack().plainText()</c>.
5341       *
5342       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5343       * <p class='bjava'>
5344       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses universal marshalling.</jc>
5345       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().universal().build();
5346       * </p>
5347       *
5348       * @return This object.
5349       */
5350      @SuppressWarnings({
5351         "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
5352      })
5353      public SELF universal() {
5354         // @formatter:off
5355         return
5356            serializers(
5357               JsonSerializer.class,
5358               JcsSerializer.class,
5359               Json5Serializer.class,
5360               Json5lSerializer.class,
5361               JsonlSerializer.class,
5362               HtmlSerializer.class,
5363               XmlSerializer.class,
5364               UonSerializer.class,
5365               UrlEncodingSerializer.class,
5366               OpenApiSerializer.class,
5367               BsonSerializer.class,
5368               CborSerializer.class,
5369               MsgPackSerializer.class,
5370               ParquetSerializer.class,
5371               PlainTextSerializer.class,
5372               TomlSerializer.class,
5373               HjsonSerializer.class,
5374               HoconSerializer.class,
5375               IniSerializer.class,
5376               PrototextSerializer.class,
5377               MarkdownSerializer.class
5378            )
5379            .parsers(
5380               JsonParser.class,
5381               Json5Parser.class,
5382               Json5lParser.class,
5383               JsonlParser.class,
5384               XmlParser.class,
5385               HtmlParser.class,
5386               UonParser.class,
5387               UrlEncodingParser.class,
5388               OpenApiParser.class,
5389               BsonParser.class,
5390               CborParser.class,
5391               MsgPackParser.class,
5392               ParquetParser.class,
5393               PlainTextParser.class,
5394               TomlParser.class,
5395               HjsonParser.class,
5396               HoconParser.class,
5397               IniParser.class,
5398               PrototextParser.class,
5399               MarkdownParser.class
5400            );
5401         // @formatter:on
5402      }

5404      /**
5405       * Convenience method for specifying UON as the marshalling transmission media type.
5406       *
5407       * <p>
5408       * UON is Url-Encoding Object notation that is equivalent to JSON but suitable for transmission as URL-encoded
5409       * query and form post values.
5410       *
5411       * <p>
5412       *    {@link UonSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
5413       *    <ul>
5414       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
5415       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5416       *    </ul>
5417       * <p>
5418       *    {@link UonParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
5419       *    <ul>
5420       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
5421       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5422       *    </ul>
5423       * <p>
5424       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/uon"</js> unless overridden
5425       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
5426       * <p>
5427       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/uon"</js> unless overridden
5428       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
5429       * <p>
5430       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
5431       *    <ul>
5432       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
5433       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
5434       *    </ul>
5435       * <p>
5436       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(UonSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(UonParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
5437       *
5438       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5439       * <p class='bjava'>
5440       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses UON marshalling.</jc>
5441       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().uon().build();
5442       * </p>
5443       *
5444       * @return This object.
5445       */
5446      public SELF uon() {
5447         return serializer(UonSerializer.class).parser(UonParser.class);
5448      }
5449
5450      /**
5451       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  URI context bean.
5452       *
5453       * <p>
5454       * Bean used for resolution of URIs to absolute or root-relative form.
5455       *
5456       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5457       * <p class='bjava'>
5458       *    <jc>// Our URI contextual information.</jc>
5459       *    String <jv>authority</jv> = <js>"http://localhost:10000"</js>;
5460       *    String <jv>contextRoot</jv> = <js>"/myContext"</js>;
5461       *    String <jv>servletPath</jv> = <js>"/myServlet"</js>;
5462       *    String <jv>pathInfo</jv> = <js>"/foo"</js>;
5463       *
5464       *    <jc>// Create a UriContext object.</jc>
5465       *    UriContext <jv>uriContext</jv> = <jk>new</jk> UriContext(<jv>authority</jv>, <jv>contextRoot</jv>, <jv>servletPath</jv>, <jv>pathInfo</jv>);
5466       *
5467       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer and associate our context.</jc>
5468       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
5469       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5470       *       .json()
5471       *       .uriContext(<jv>uriContext</jv>)
5472       *       .uriRelativity(<jsf>RESOURCE</jsf>)  <jc>// Assume relative paths are relative to servlet.</jc>
5473       *       .uriResolution(<jsf>ABSOLUTE</jsf>)  <jc>// Serialize URIs as absolute paths.</jc>
5474       *       .build();
5475       *
5476       *    <jc>// A relative URI</jc>
5477       *    URI <jv>uri</jv> = <jk>new</jk> URI(<js>"bar"</js>);
5478       *
5479       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  "http://localhost:10000/myContext/myServlet/foo/bar"</jc>
5480       *    <jv>client</jv>
5481       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jv>uri</jv>)
5482       *       .run();
5483       * </p>
5484       *
5485       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5486       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#uriContext(UriContext)}
5487       *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshallingUris">URIs</a>
5488       * </ul>
5489       *
5490       * @param value The new value for this property.
5491       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
5492       * @return This object.
5493       */
5494      public SELF uriContext(UriContext value) {
5495         serializers().forEach(x -> x.uriContext(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)));
5496         return self();
5497      }
5498
5499      /**
5500       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  URI relativity.
5501       *
5502       * <p>
5503       * Defines what relative URIs are relative to when serializing any of the following:
5504       * <ul>
5505       *    <li>{@link URI}
5506       *    <li>{@link URL}
5507       *    <li>Properties and classes annotated with {@link Uri @Uri}
5508       * </ul>
5509       *
5510       * <p>
5511       * See {@link #uriContext(UriContext)} for examples.
5512       *
5513       * <ul class='values javatree'>
5514       *    <li class='jf'>{@link UriRelativity#RESOURCE}
5515       *       - Relative URIs should be considered relative to the servlet URI.
5516       *    <li class='jf'>{@link UriRelativity#PATH_INFO}
5517       *       - Relative URIs should be considered relative to the request URI.
5518       * </ul>
5519       *
5520       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5521       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#uriRelativity(UriRelativity)}
5522       *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshallingUris">URIs</a>
5523       * </ul>
5524       *
5525       * @param value
5526       *    The new value for this property.
5527       *    <br>The default is {@link UriRelativity#RESOURCE}
5528       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
5529       * @return This object.
5530       */
5531      public SELF uriRelativity(UriRelativity value) {
5532         serializers().forEach(x -> x.uriRelativity(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)));
5533         return self();
5534      }
5535
5536      /**
5537       * <i><l>Serializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  URI resolution.
5538       *
5539       * <p>
5540       * Defines the resolution level for URIs when serializing any of the following:
5541       * <ul>
5542       *    <li>{@link URI}
5543       *    <li>{@link URL}
5544       *    <li>Properties and classes annotated with {@link Uri @Uri}
5545       * </ul>
5546       *
5547       * <p>
5548       * See {@link #uriContext(UriContext)} for examples.
5549       *
5550       * <ul class='values'>
5551       *    <li class='jf'>{@link UriResolution#ABSOLUTE}
5552       *       - Resolve to an absolute URI (e.g. <js>"http://host:port/context-root/servlet-path/path-info"</js>).
5553       *    <li class='jf'>{@link UriResolution#ROOT_RELATIVE}
5554       *       - Resolve to a root-relative URI (e.g. <js>"/context-root/servlet-path/path-info"</js>).
5555       *    <li class='jf'>{@link UriResolution#NONE}
5556       *       - Don't do any URI resolution.
5557       * </ul>
5558       *
5559       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5560       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer.Builder#uriResolution(UriResolution)}
5561       *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshallingUris">URIs</a>
5562       * </ul>
5563       *
5564       * @param value
5565       *    The new value for this property.
5566       *    <br>The default is {@link UriResolution#NONE}
5567       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
5568       * @return This object.
5569       */
5570      public SELF uriResolution(UriResolution value) {
5571         serializers().forEach(x -> x.uriResolution(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value)));
5572         return self();
5573      }
5574
5575      /**
5576       * Convenience method for specifying URL-Encoding as the marshalling transmission media type.
5577       *
5578       * <p>
5579       *    {@link UrlEncodingSerializer} will be used to serialize POJOs to request bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#serializer(Serializer)}.
5580       *    <ul>
5581       *       <li>The serializer can be configured using any of the serializer property setters (e.g. {@link #sortCollections()}) or
5582       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5583       *       <li>This serializer is NOT used when using the {@link RestRequest#formData(String, Object)} (and related) methods for constructing
5584       *          the request body.  Instead, the part serializer specified via {@link #partSerializer(Class)} is used.
5585       *    </ul>
5586       * <p>
5587       *    {@link UrlEncodingParser} will be used to parse POJOs from response bodies unless overridden per request via {@link RestRequest#parser(Parser)}.
5588       *    <ul>
5589       *       <li>The parser can be configured using any of the parser property setters (e.g. {@link #ignoreUnknownBeanProperties()}) or
5590       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5591       *    </ul>
5592       * <p>
5593       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"</js> unless overridden
5594       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
5595       * <p>
5596       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"</js> unless overridden
5597       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
5598       * <p>
5599       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
5600       *    <ul>
5601       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
5602       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
5603       *    </ul>
5604       * <p>
5605       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(UrlEncodingSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(UrlEncodingParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
5606       *
5607       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5608       * <p class='bjava'>
5609       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses URL-Encoded marshalling.</jc>
5610       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>create</jsm>().urlEnc().build();
5611       * </p>
5612       *
5613       * @return This object.
5614       */
5615      public SELF urlEnc() {
5616         return serializer(UrlEncodingSerializer.class).parser(UrlEncodingParser.class);
5617      }
5618
5619      /**
5620       * Returns the URL-encoding serializer sub-builder.
5621       *
5622       * @return The URL-encoding serializer sub-builder.
5623       */
5624      public final UrlEncodingSerializer.Builder urlEncodingSerializer() {
5625         if (urlEncodingSerializer == null)
5626            urlEncodingSerializer = createUrlEncodingSerializer();
5627         return urlEncodingSerializer;
5628      }

5659      /**
5660       * <i><l>WriterSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Use whitespace.
5661       *
5662       * <p>
5663       * When enabled, whitespace is added to the output to improve readability.
5664       *
5665       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5666       * <p class='bjava'>
5667       *    <jc>// Create a REST client with JSON serializer with whitespace enabled.</jc>
5668       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient
5669       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
5670       *       .json()
5671       *       .useWhitespace()
5672       *       .build();
5673       *
5674       *    <jc>// A bean with a single property</jc>
5675       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBean {
5676       *       <jk>public</jk> String <jf>foo</jf> = <js>"bar"</js>;
5677       *    }
5678       *
5679       *    <jc>// Request body will contain:  {\n\t"foo": "bar"\n\}\n</jc>
5680       *    <jv>client</jv>
5681       *       .post(<js>"http://localhost:10000/foo"</js>, <jk>new</jk> MyBean())
5682       *       .run();
5683       * </p>
5684       *
5685       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
5686       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializer.Builder#useWhitespace()}
5687       * </ul>
5688       * @return This object.
5689       */
5690      public SELF useWhitespace() {
5691         serializers().forEachWS(WriterSerializer.Builder::useWhitespace);
5692         return self();
5693      }
5694

5718       *       .run();

5720       *

5723       * </ul>

5727      public SELF ws() {

5737       *    <ul>

5739       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5740       *    </ul>

5743       *    <ul>

5745       *          bean context property setters (e.g. {@link #swaps(Class...)}) defined on this builder class.
5746       *    </ul>
5747       * <p>
5748       *    <c>Accept</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/xml"</js> unless overridden
5749       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
5750       * <p>
5751       *    <c>Content-Type</c> request header will be set to <js>"text/xml"</js> unless overridden
5752       *       via {@link #headers()}, or per-request via {@link RestRequest#header(Header)}.
5753       * <p>
5754       *    Can be combined with other marshaller setters such as {@link #json()} to provide support for multiple languages.
5755       *    <ul>
5756       *       <li>When multiple languages are supported, the <c>Accept</c> and <c>Content-Type</c> headers control which marshallers are used, or uses the
5757       *       last-enabled language if the headers are not set.
5758       *    </ul>
5759       * <p>
5760       *    Identical to calling <c>serializer(XmlSerializer.<jk>class</jk>).parser(XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>)</c>.
5761       *
5762       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5763       * <p class='bjava'>
5764       *    <jc>// Construct a client that uses XML marshalling.</jc>

5766       * </p>

5774      /**
5775       * Creates the creator for the rest call handler.
5776       *
5777       * <p>
5778       * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own implementation.
5779       *
5780       * <p>
5781       * The default behavior creates a bean instantiator initialized to return a {@link BasicRestCallHandler}.
5782       *
5783       * @return The creator for the rest call handler.
5784       * @see #callHandler()
5785       */
5786      protected BeanInstantiator.Builder<RestCallHandler> createCallHandler() {
5787         return BeanInstantiator.of(RestCallHandler.class, beanStore).type(BasicRestCallHandler.class);

5790      /**
5791       * Creates the {@link HttpClientConnectionManager} returned by {@link #createConnectionManager()}.
5792       *
5793       * <p>
5794       * Subclasses can override this method to provide their own connection manager.
5795       *
5796       * <p>
5797       * The default implementation returns an instance of a {@link PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager} if {@link #pooled()}
5798       * was called or {@link BasicHttpClientConnectionManager} if not..
5799       *
5800       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5801       * <p class='bjava'>
5802       *    <jc>// A Builder that provides it's own customized HttpClientConnectionManager.</jc>
5803       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBuilder <jk>extends</jk> Builder {
5804       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
5805       *       <jk>protected</jk> HttpClientConnectionManager createConnectionManager() {
5806       *          <jk>return new</jk> PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager();
5807       *       }
5808       *    }
5809       *
5810       *    <jc>// Instantiate.</jc>
5811       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyBuilder().build();
5812       * </p>
5813       *
5814       * @return The HTTP client builder to use to create the HTTP client.
5815       */
5816      protected HttpClientConnectionManager createConnectionManager() {

5852      /**
5853       * Creates an instance of an {@link HttpClient} to be used to handle all HTTP communications with the target server.
5854       *
5855       * <p>
5856       * This HTTP client is used when the HTTP client is not specified through one of the constructors or the
5857       * {@link #httpClient(CloseableHttpClient)} method.
5858       *
5859       * <p>
5860       * Subclasses can override this method to provide specially-configured HTTP clients to handle stuff such as
5861       * SSL/TLS certificate handling, authentication, etc.
5862       *
5863       * <p>
5864       * The default implementation returns an instance of {@link HttpClient} using the client builder returned by
5865       * {@link #createHttpClientBuilder()}.
5866       *
5867       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
5868       * <p class='bjava'>
5869       *    <jc>// A Builder that provides it's own customized HttpClient.</jc>
5870       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyBuilder <jk>extends</jk> Builder {
5871       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
5872       *       <jk>protected</jk> HttpClientBuilder createHttpClient() {
5873       *          <jk>return</jk> HttpClientBuilder.<jsm>create</jsm>().build();
5874       *       }
5875       *    }
5876       *
5877       *    <jc>// Instantiate.</jc>
5878       *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MyBuilder().build();
5879       * </p>
5880       *
5881       * @return The HTTP client to use.
5882       */
5883      protected CloseableHttpClient createHttpClient() {
5884         if (connectionManager == null)
5885            connectionManager = createConnectionManager();
5886         httpClientBuilder().setConnectionManager(connectionManager);
5887         return httpClientBuilder().build();
5888      }

5906       *

5909       * </p>
5910       *
5911       * @return The HTTP client builder to use to create the HTTP client.
5912       */
5913      protected HttpClientBuilder createHttpClientBuilder() {
5914         return HttpClientBuilder.create();
5915      }

6058   /**
6059    * Constructor.
6060    *
6061    * @param builder The builder for this client.
6062    */
6063   public RestClient(Builder<?> builder) {
6064      super(builder);
6065
6066      beanStore = builder.beanStore.addBean(RestClient.class, this);
6067
6068      callHandler = builder.callHandler().run();
6069      connectionManager = builder.connectionManager;
6070      console = nn(builder.console) ? builder.console : System.err;
6071      creationStack = isDebug() ? Thread.currentThread().getStackTrace() : null;
6072      detectLeaks = builder.detectLeaks;
6073      errorCodes = builder.errorCodes;
6074      formData = builder.formData().copy();
6075      headerData = builder.headers().copy();
6076      httpClient = builder.getHttpClient();
6077      ignoreErrors = builder.ignoreErrors;
6078      interceptors = nn(builder.interceptors) ? builder.interceptors.toArray(EMPTY_REST_CALL_INTERCEPTORS) : EMPTY_REST_CALL_INTERCEPTORS;
6079      keepHttpClientOpen = builder.keepHttpClientOpen;
6080      logger = nn(builder.logger) ? builder.logger : Logger.getLogger(cn(RestClient.class));
6081      if (nn(builder.logRequests)) {
6082         logRequests = builder.logRequests;
6083      } else {
6084         logRequests = isDebug() ? DetailLevel.FULL : DetailLevel.NONE;
6085      }
6086      if (nn(builder.logRequestsLevel)) {
6087         logRequestsLevel = builder.logRequestsLevel;
6088      } else {
6089         logRequestsLevel = isDebug() ? Level.WARNING : Level.OFF;
6090      }
6091      logRequestsPredicate = nn(builder.logRequestsPredicate) ? builder.logRequestsPredicate : LOG_REQUESTS_PREDICATE_DEFAULT;
6092      logToConsole = builder.logToConsole || isDebug();
6093      parsers = builder.parsers().build();
6094      partParser = builder.partParser().create();
6095      partSerializer = builder.partSerializer().create();
6096      pathData = builder.pathData().copy();
6097      queryData = builder.queryData().copy();
6098      rootUrl = builder.rootUrl;
6099      serializers = builder.serializers().build();
6100      skipEmptyFormData = builder.skipEmptyFormData;
6101      skipEmptyHeaderData = builder.skipEmptyHeaderData;
6102      skipEmptyQueryData = builder.skipEmptyQueryData;
6103      urlEncodingSerializer = builder.urlEncodingSerializer().build();
6104      if (builder.executorService != null)
6105         executorService.set(builder.executorService);
6106      executorServiceShutdownOnClose = builder.executorServiceShutdownOnClose;
6107
6108      init();
6109   }

6120   /**
6121    * Performs a REST call where the entire call is specified in a simple string.
6122    *
6123    * <p>
6124    * This method is useful for performing callbacks when the target of a callback is passed in
6125    * on an initial request, for example to signal when a long-running process has completed.
6126    *
6127    * <p>
6128    * The call string can be any of the following formats:
6129    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
6130    *    <li>
6131    *       <js>"[method] [uri]"</js> - e.g. <js>"GET http://localhost/callback"</js>
6132    *    <li>
6133    *       <js>"[method] [uri] [payload]"</js> - e.g. <js>"POST http://localhost/callback some text payload"</js>
6134    *    <li>
6135    *       <js>"[method] [headers] [uri] [payload]"</js> - e.g. <js>"POST {'Content-Type':'text/json'} http://localhost/callback {'some':'json'}"</js>
6136    * </ul>
6137    * <p>
6138    * The payload will always be sent using a simple {@link StringEntity}.
6139    *
6140    * @param callString The call string.
6141    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> or empty (treated as empty string, will result in an invalid request).
6142    * @return
6143    *    A {@link RestRequest} object that can be further tailored before executing the request and getting the response
6144    *    as a parsed object.
6145    * @throws RestCallException REST call failed.
6146    */
6147   @SuppressWarnings({
6148      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for callback parsing state machine
6149      "java:S1854", // state = S5 is a necessary state machine transition; SonarLint false positive
6150      "java:S4165"  // state already holds S5 in some paths, but the assignment is a deliberate state machine transition
6151   })
6152   public RestRequest callback(String callString) throws RestCallException {
6153      callString = emptyIfNull(callString);
6154
6155      // S1 - Looking for end of method.
6156      // S2 - Found end of method, looking for beginning of URI or headers.
6157      // S3 - Found beginning of headers, looking for end of headers.
6158      // S4 - Found end of headers, looking for beginning of URI.
6159      // S5 - Found beginning of URI, looking for end of URI.
6160
6161      var state = S1;
6162
6163      var mark = 0;
6164      String method = null;
6165      String headers = null;
6166      String uri = null;
6167      String content = null;
6168      for (var i = 0; i < callString.length(); i++) {
6169         var c = callString.charAt(i);
6170         if (state == S1) {
6171            if (isWhitespace(c)) {
6172               method = callString.substring(mark, i);
6173               state = S2;
6174            }
6175         } else if (state == S2) {
6176            if (! isWhitespace(c)) {
6177               mark = i;
6178               state = (c == '{') ? S3 : S5;
6179            }
6180         } else if (state == S3) {
6181            if (c == '}') {
6182               headers = callString.substring(mark, i + 1);
6183               state = S4;
6184            }
6185         } else if (state == S4) {
6186            if (! isWhitespace(c)) {
6187               mark = i;
6188               state = S5;
6189            }
6190         } else /* (state == S5) */ {
6191            if (isWhitespace(c)) {
6192               uri = callString.substring(mark, i);
6193               content = callString.substring(i).trim();
6194               break;
6195            }
6196         }
6197      }
6198
6199      if (state != S5)
6200         throw new RestCallException(null, null, "Invalid format for call string.  State={0}", state);
6201
6202      try {
6203         var req = request(method, uri, ine(content));
6204         if (nn(headers))
6205            Json5Map.ofString(headers).forEach((k, v) -> req.header(stringHeader(k, s(v))));
6206         if (ine(content))
6207            req.contentString(content);
6208         return req;
6209      } catch (ParseException e) {
6210         throw new RestCallException(null, e, "Invalid format for call string.");
6211      }
6212   }
6213
6214   /**
6215    * Calls {@link CloseableHttpClient#close()} on the underlying {@link CloseableHttpClient}.
6216    *
6217    * <p>
6218    * It's good practice to call this method after the client is no longer used.
6219    *
6220    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
6221    */
6222   @Override
6223   public void close() throws IOException {
6224      isClosed.set(true);
6225      ExecutorService es = executorService.get();
6226      if (! keepHttpClientOpen)
6227         httpClient.close();
6228      if (nn(es) && executorServiceShutdownOnClose)
6229         es.shutdown();
6230      // If executorServiceShutdownOnClose is false, executor service remains open for reuse
6231      if (nn(creationStack))
6232         closedStack = Thread.currentThread().getStackTrace();
6233   }
6234
6235   /**
6236    * Same as {@link #close()}, but ignores any exceptions.
6237    */
6238   public void closeQuietly() {
6239      isClosed.set(true);
6240      try {
6241         if (! keepHttpClientOpen)
6242            httpClient.close();
6243         ExecutorService es = executorService.get();
6244         if (nn(es) && executorServiceShutdownOnClose)
6245            es.shutdown();
6246      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Exception t) { /* Suppress close/shutdown exceptions during cleanup */ }
6247      if (nn(creationStack))
6248         closedStack = Thread.currentThread().getStackTrace();
6249   }

6389   /**

6391    *
6392    * <p>
6393    * The content entity associated with the response is fully consumed and the underlying connection is released back
6394    * to the connection manager automatically in all cases relieving individual {@link ResponseHandler ResponseHandlers}
6395    * from having to manage resource deallocation internally.
6396    *
6397    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
6398    *    <li class='note'>This method gets passed on directly to the underlying {@link HttpClient} class.
6399    * </ul>
6400    *
6401    * @param target
6402    *    The target host for the request.
6403    *    <br>Implementations may accept <jk>null</jk> if they can still determine a route, for example to a default target or by inspecting the request.
6404    * @param request The request to execute.
6405    * @param responseHandler The response handler.
6406    * @return The response object as generated by the response handler.
6407    * @throws IOException In case of a problem or the connection was aborted.
6408    * @throws ClientProtocolException In case of an http protocol error.
6409    */
6410   @Override /* Overridden from HttpClient */
6411   public <T> T execute(HttpHost target, HttpRequest request, ResponseHandler<? extends T> responseHandler) throws IOException {
6412      return httpClient.execute(target, request, responseHandler);
6413   }

6415   /**
6416    * Executes a request using the default context and processes the response using the given response handler.
6417    *
6418    * <p>
6419    * The content entity associated with the response is fully consumed and the underlying connection is released back
6420    * to the connection manager automatically in all cases relieving individual {@link ResponseHandler ResponseHandlers}
6421    * from having to manage resource deallocation internally.
6422    *
6423    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
6424    *    <li class='note'>This method gets passed on directly to the underlying {@link HttpClient} class.
6425    * </ul>
6426    *

6554   /**
6555    * Perform a <c>POST</c> request with a content type of <c>application/x-www-form-urlencoded</c>
6556    * against the specified URI.
6557    *
6558    * @param uri
6559    *    The URI of the remote REST resource.
6560    *    <br>Can be any of the following types:
6561    *    <ul>
6562    *       <li>{@link URIBuilder}
6563    *       <li>{@link URI}
6564    *       <li>{@link URL}
6565    *       <li>{@link String}
6566    *       <li>{@link Object} - Converted to <c>String</c> using <c>toString()</c>
6567    *    </ul>
6568    * @param body
6569    *    The object to serialize and transmit to the URI as the body of the request.
6570    *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
6571    *       <li>{@link NameValuePair} - URL-encoded as a single name-value pair.
6572    *       <li>{@link NameValuePair} array - URL-encoded as name value pairs.
6573    *       <li>{@link PartList} - URL-encoded as name value pairs.
6574    *       <li>{@link Reader}/{@link InputStream}- Streamed directly and <l>Content-Type</l> set to <js>"application/x-www-form-urlencoded"</js>
6575    *       <li>{@link HttpResource} - Raw contents will be serialized to remote resource.  Additional headers and media type will be set on request.
6576    *       <li>{@link HttpEntity} - Bypass Juneau serialization and pass HttpEntity directly to HttpClient.
6577    *       <li>{@link Object} - Converted to a {@link SerializedEntity} using {@link UrlEncodingSerializer} to serialize.
6578    *       <li>{@link Supplier} - A supplier of anything on this list.
6579    *    </ul>
6580    * @return
6581    *    A {@link RestRequest} object that can be further tailored before executing the request and getting the response
6582    *    as a parsed object.
6583    * @throws RestCallException If any authentication errors occurred.
6584    */
6585   public RestRequest formPost(Object uri, Object body) throws RestCallException {
6586      var req = request(op("POST", uri, NO_BODY));
6587      try {
6588         if (body instanceof Supplier<?> body2)
6589            body = body2.get();
6590         if (body instanceof NameValuePair body2)
6591            return req.content(new UrlEncodedFormEntity(l(body2)));
6592         if (body instanceof NameValuePair[] namevaluepairArray)
6593            return req.content(new UrlEncodedFormEntity(l(namevaluepairArray)));
6594         if (body instanceof PartList body2)
6595            return req.content(new UrlEncodedFormEntity(body2));
6596         if (body instanceof HttpResource body2)
6597            body2.getHeaders().forEach(req::header);
6598         if (body instanceof HttpEntity body2) {
6599            if (body2.getContentType() == null)
6600               req.header(ContentType.APPLICATION_FORM_URLENCODED);
6601            return req.content(body2);
6602         }
6603         if (body instanceof Reader || body instanceof InputStream)
6604            return req.header(ContentType.APPLICATION_FORM_URLENCODED).content(body);
6605         return req.content(serializedEntity(body, urlEncodingSerializer, null));
6606      } catch (IOException e) {
6607         throw new RestCallException(null, e, "Could not read form post body.");
6608      }
6609   }

6707   /**
6708    * Create a new proxy interface against a 3rd-party REST interface.
6709    *
6710    * <p>
6711    * The URI to the REST interface is based on the following values:
6712    * <ul>
6713    *    <li>The {@link Remote#path() @Remote(path)} annotation on the interface (<c>remote-path</c>).
6714    *    <li>The {@link Builder#rootUrl(Object) rootUrl} on the client (<c>root-url</c>).
6715    *    <li>The fully-qualified class name of the interface (<c>class-name</c>).
6716    * </ul>
6717    *
6718    * <p>
6719    * The URI calculation is as follows:
6720    * <ul>
6721    *    <li><c>remote-path</c> - If remote path is absolute.
6722    *    <li><c>root-uri/remote-path</c> - If remote path is relative and root-uri has been specified.
6723    *    <li><c>root-uri/class-name</c> - If remote path is not specified.
6724    * </ul>
6725    *
6726    * <p>
6727    * If the information is not available to resolve to an absolute URI, a {@link RemoteMetadataException} is thrown.
6728    *
6729    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
6730    * <p class='bjava'>
6731    *    <jk>package</jk> org.apache.foo;
6732    *
6733    *    <ja>@RemoteResource</ja>(path=<js>"http://hostname/resturi/myinterface1"</js>)
6734    *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyInterface1 { ... }
6735    *
6736    *    <ja>@RemoteResource</ja>(path=<js>"/myinterface2"</js>)
6737    *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyInterface2 { ... }
6738    *
6739    *    <jk>public interface</jk> MyInterface3 { ... }
6740    *
6741    *    <jc>// Resolves to "http://localhost/resturi/myinterface1"</jc>
6742    *    MyInterface1 <jv>interface1</jv> = RestClient
6743    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
6744    *       .build()
6745    *       .getRemote(MyInterface1.<jk>class</jk>);
6746    *
6747    *    <jc>// Resolves to "http://hostname/resturi/myinterface2"</jc>
6748    *    MyInterface2 <jv>interface2</jv> = RestClient
6749    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
6750    *       .rootUrl(<js>"http://hostname/resturi"</js>)
6751    *       .build()
6752    *       .getRemote(MyInterface2.<jk>class</jk>);
6753    *
6754    *    <jc>// Resolves to "http://hostname/resturi/org.apache.foo.MyInterface3"</jc>
6755    *    MyInterface3 <jv>interface3</jv> = RestClient
6756    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
6757    *       .rootUrl(<js>"http://hostname/resturi"</js>)
6758    *       .build()
6759    *       .getRemote(MyInterface3.<jk>class</jk>);
6760    * </p>
6761    *
6762    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
6763    *    <li class='note'>
6764    *       If you plan on using your proxy in a multi-threaded environment, you'll want to use an underlying
6765    *       pooling client connection manager.
6766    * </ul>
6767    *
6768    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
6769    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestProxies">REST Proxy Basics</a>
6770    * </ul>
6771    *
6772    * @param <T> The interface to create a proxy for.
6773    * @param interfaceClass The interface to create a proxy for.
6774    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
6775    * @return The new proxy interface.
6776    * @throws RemoteMetadataException If the REST URI cannot be determined based on the information given.
6777    */
6778   public <T> T getRemote(Class<T> interfaceClass) {
6779      return getRemote(interfaceClass, null);
6780   }
6781

6783    * Same as {@link #getRemote(Class)} except explicitly specifies the URI of the REST interface.

6787    * </ul>
6788    *
6789    * @param <T> The interface to create a proxy for.
6790    * @param interfaceClass The interface to create a proxy for.
6791    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
6792    * @param rootUrl The URI of the REST interface.
6793    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the root URL from the client builder if set).
6794    * @return The new proxy interface.
6795    */
6796   public <T> T getRemote(Class<T> interfaceClass, Object rootUrl) {
6797      return getRemote(interfaceClass, rootUrl, null, null);
6798   }

6800   /**
6801    * Same as {@link #getRemote(Class, Object)} but allows you to override the serializer and parser used.
6802    *
6803    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
6804    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestProxies">REST Proxy Basics</a>
6805    * </ul>
6806
6807    * @param <T> The interface to create a proxy for.
6808    * @param interfaceClass The interface to create a proxy for.
6809    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
6810    * @param rootUrl The URI of the REST interface.
6811    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the root URL from the client builder if set).
6812    * @param serializer The serializer used to serialize POJOs to the body of the HTTP request.
6813    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the default serializer from the client).
6814    * @param parser The parser used to parse POJOs from the body of the HTTP response.
6815    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the default parser from the client).
6816    * @return The new proxy interface.
