6818   @SuppressWarnings({
6819      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
6820      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for remote proxy creation
6821      "java:S6541" // Brain method acceptable - remote proxy creation requires complex initialization logic
6822   })
6823   public <T> T getRemote(Class<T> interfaceClass, Object rootUrl, Serializer serializer, Parser parser) {
6824
6825      if (rootUrl == null)
6826         rootUrl = this.rootUrl != null ? this.rootUrl.get() : null;
6827
6828      final String restUrl2 = trimSlashes(emptyIfNull(rootUrl));
6829
6830      return (T)Proxy.newProxyInstance(interfaceClass.getClassLoader(), a(interfaceClass), new InvocationHandler() {
6831
6832         final RemoteMeta rm = new RemoteMeta(interfaceClass);
6833
6834         @Override /* Overridden from InvocationHandler */
6835      @SuppressWarnings({
6836         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for proxy invocation handler
6837      })
6838      public Object invoke(Object proxy, Method method, Object[] args) throws Throwable {
6839            var rom = rm.getOperationMeta(method);
6840
6841            var uri = rom.getFullPath();
6842            if (uri.indexOf("://") == -1)
6843               uri = restUrl2 + '/' + uri;
6844            if (uri.indexOf("://") == -1)
6845               throw new RemoteMetadataException(interfaceClass, "Root URI has not been specified.  Cannot construct absolute path to remote resource.");
6846
6847            var httpMethod = rom.getHttpMethod();
6848            var rc = request(httpMethod, uri, hasContent(httpMethod));
6849
6850            if (serializer != null) rc.serializer(serializer);
6851            if (parser != null) rc.parser(parser);
6852
6853            rm.getHeaders().forEach(rc::header);
6854
6855            // Apply method-level defaults if parameter values are not provided (9.2.0)
6856            rom.forEachPathArg(a -> {
6857               var val = args[a.getIndex()];
6858               if (val == null) {
6859                  // Check parameter-level default first (9.2.0)
6860                  var def = a.getSchema().getDefault();
6861                  // Fall back to method-level default if parameter-level not set
6862                  if (def == null)
6863                     def = rom.getPathDefault(a.getName());
6864                  if (nn(def))
6865                     val = def;
6866               }
6867               rc.pathArg(a.getName(), val, a.getSchema(), a.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer));
6868            });
6869            rom.forEachQueryArg(a -> {
6870               var val = args[a.getIndex()];
6871               if (val == null) {
6872                  // Check parameter-level default first (9.2.0)
6873                  var def = a.getSchema().getDefault();
6874                  // Fall back to method-level default if parameter-level not set
6875                  if (def == null)
6876                     def = rom.getQueryDefault(a.getName());
6877                  if (nn(def))
6878                     val = def;
6879               }
6880               rc.queryArg(a.getName(), val, a.getSchema(), a.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer), a.isSkipIfEmpty());
6881            });
6882            rom.forEachFormDataArg(a -> {
6883               var val = args[a.getIndex()];
6884               if (val == null) {
6885                  // Check parameter-level default first (9.2.0)
6886                  var def = a.getSchema().getDefault();
6887                  // Fall back to method-level default if parameter-level not set
6888                  if (def == null)
6889                     def = rom.getFormDataDefault(a.getName());
6890                  if (nn(def))
6891                     val = def;
6892               }
6893               rc.formDataArg(a.getName(), val, a.getSchema(), a.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer), a.isSkipIfEmpty());
6894            });
6895            rom.forEachHeaderArg(a -> {
6896               var val = args[a.getIndex()];
6897               if (val == null) {
6898                  // Check parameter-level default first (9.2.0)
6899                  var def = a.getSchema().getDefault();
6900                  // Fall back to method-level default if parameter-level not set
6901                  if (def == null)
6902                     def = rom.getHeaderDefault(a.getName());
6903                  if (nn(def))
6904                     val = def;
6905               }
6906               rc.headerArg(a.getName(), val, a.getSchema(), a.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer), a.isSkipIfEmpty());
6907            });
6908
6909            var ba = rom.getContentArg();
6910            if (nn(ba)) {
6911               var val = args[ba.getIndex()];
6912               if (val == null) {
6913                  // Check parameter-level default first (9.2.0)
6914                  var def = ba.getSchema().getDefault();
6915                  // Fall back to method-level default if parameter-level not set
6916                  if (def == null)
6917                     def = rom.getContentDefault();
6918                  if (nn(def))
6919                     val = def;
6920               }
6921               rc.content(val, ba.getSchema());
6922            } else {
6923               // Apply Content default if no parameter is present
6924               var contentDef = rom.getContentDefault();
6925               if (nn(contentDef))
6926                  rc.content(contentDef);
6927            }
6928
6929            rom.forEachRequestArg(rmba -> {
6930               var rbm = rmba.getMeta();
6931               var bean = args[rmba.getIndex()];
6932               if (nn(bean)) {
6933                  for (var p : rbm.getProperties()) {
6934                     var val = safeSupplier(() -> p.getGetter().invoke(bean));

6936                     var pn = p.getPartName();
6937                     var schema = p.getSchema();
6938                     if (pt == PATH)

6940                     else if (nn(val)) {
6941                        if (pt == QUERY)
6942                           rc.queryArg(pn, val, schema, p.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer), schema.isSkipIfEmpty());
6943                        else if (pt == FORMDATA)
6944                           rc.formDataArg(pn, val, schema, p.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer), schema.isSkipIfEmpty());
6945                        else if (pt == HEADER)
6946                           rc.headerArg(pn, val, schema, p.getSerializer().orElse(partSerializer), schema.isSkipIfEmpty());

6948                           rc.content(val, schema);

6950                  }
6951               }
6952            });
6953
6954            var ror = rom.getReturns();
6955            if (ror.isFuture()) {
6956            return getExecutorService().submit(() -> {
6957               try {
6958                  return executeRemote(interfaceClass, rc, method, rom);
6959               } catch (Exception e) {
6960                  throw toRex(e);
6961               }
6962            });
6963            } else if (ror.isCompletableFuture()) {
6964               var cf = new CompletableFuture<>();
6965               getExecutorService().submit(() -> {
6966                  try {
6967                     cf.complete(executeRemote(interfaceClass, rc, method, rom));
6968                  } catch (Exception e) {
6969                     cf.completeExceptionally(e);
6970                  }
6971                  return null;
6972               });
6973               return cf;
6974            }
6975
6976            try {
6977               return executeRemote(interfaceClass, rc, method, rom);
6978            } catch (Exception e) {
6979               // Check if this is a RuntimeException wrapping a RestCallException with an Error cause
6980               if (e instanceof RuntimeException rex && rex.getCause() instanceof RestCallException rce) {
6981                  var t = rce.getCause();
6982                  if (nn(t)) {
6983                     // Check if the cause matches the method's declared exception types
6984                     for (var t2 : method.getExceptionTypes())
6985                        if (t2.isInstance(t))
6986                           throw t; // Rethrow the original Throwable (can be Error)
6987                  }
6988               }
6989               throw e;
6990            }
6991         }
6992      });
6993   }
6994
6995   /**
6996    * Create a new proxy interface against an RRPC-style service.
6997    *
6998    * <p>
6999    * Remote interfaces are interfaces exposed on the server side using either the <c>RrpcServlet</c>
7000    * or <c>RRPC</c> REST methods.
7001    *
7002    * <p>
7003    * The URI to the REST interface is based on the following values:
7004    * <ul>
7005    *    <li>The {@link Remote#path() @Remote(path)} annotation on the interface (<c>remote-path</c>).
7006    *    <li>The {@link Builder#rootUrl(Object) rootUrl} on the client (<c>root-url</c>).
7007    *    <li>The fully-qualified class name of the interface (<c>class-name</c>).
7008    * </ul>
7009    *
7010    * <p>
7011    * The URI calculation is as follows:
7012    * <ul>
7013    *    <li><c>remote-path</c> - If remote path is absolute.
7014    *    <li><c>root-url/remote-path</c> - If remote path is relative and root-url has been specified.
7015    *    <li><c>root-url/class-name</c> - If remote path is not specified.
7016    * </ul>
7017    *
7018    * <p>
7019    * If the information is not available to resolve to an absolute URI, a {@link RemoteMetadataException} is thrown.
7020    *
7021    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
7022    *    <li class='note'>
7023    *       If you plan on using your proxy in a multi-threaded environment, you'll want to use an underlying
7024    *       pooling client connection manager.
7025    * </ul>
7026    *
7027    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
7028    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestRpc">REST/RPC</a>
7029    * </ul>
7030    *
7031    * @param <T> The interface to create a proxy for.
7032    * @param interfaceClass The interface to create a proxy for.
7033    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
7034    * @return The new proxy interface.
7035    * @throws RemoteMetadataException If the REST URI cannot be determined based on the information given.
7036    */
7037   public <T> T getRrpcInterface(Class<T> interfaceClass) {
7038      return getRrpcInterface(interfaceClass, null);
7039   }
7040
7041   /**
7042    * Same as {@link #getRrpcInterface(Class)} except explicitly specifies the URI of the REST interface.
7043    *
7044    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
7045    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestRpc">REST/RPC</a>
7046    * </ul>
7047    *
7048    * @param <T> The interface to create a proxy for.
7049    * @param interfaceClass The interface to create a proxy for.
7050    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
7051    * @param uri The URI of the REST interface.
7052    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the root URL from the client builder if set).
7053    * @return The new proxy interface.
7054    */
7055   public <T> T getRrpcInterface(Class<T> interfaceClass, Object uri) {
7056      return getRrpcInterface(interfaceClass, uri, null, null);
7057   }
7058
7059   /**
7060    * Same as {@link #getRrpcInterface(Class, Object)} but allows you to override the serializer and parser used.
7061    *
7062    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
7063    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestRpc">REST/RPC</a>
7064    * </ul>
7065    *
7066    * @param <T> The interface to create a proxy for.
7067    * @param interfaceClass The interface to create a proxy for.
7068    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
7069    * @param uri The URI of the REST interface.
7070    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the root URL from the client builder if set).
7071    * @param serializer The serializer used to serialize POJOs to the body of the HTTP request.
7072    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the default serializer from the client).
7073    * @param parser The parser used to parse POJOs from the body of the HTTP response.
7074    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will use the default parser from the client).
7075    * @return The new proxy interface.
7076    */
7077   @SuppressWarnings({
7078      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked casts in REST client operations
7079      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for this specific logic
7080   })
7081   public <T> T getRrpcInterface(Class<T> interfaceClass, Object uri, Serializer serializer, Parser parser) {
7082
7083      if (uri == null) {
7084         var rm = new RrpcInterfaceMeta(interfaceClass, "");
7085         var path = rm.getPath();
7086         if (path.indexOf("://") == -1) {
7087            var rootUrlValue = rootUrl != null ? rootUrl.get() : null;
7088            if (isEmpty(rootUrlValue))
7089               throw new RemoteMetadataException(interfaceClass, "Root URI has not been specified.  Cannot construct absolute path to remote interface.");
7090            path = trimSlashes(rootUrlValue) + '/' + path;
7091         }
7092         uri = path;
7093      }
7094
7095      final String restUrl2 = s(uri);
7096
7097      return (T)Proxy.newProxyInstance(interfaceClass.getClassLoader(), a(interfaceClass), new InvocationHandler() {
7098
7099         final RrpcInterfaceMeta rm = new RrpcInterfaceMeta(interfaceClass, restUrl2);
7100
7101         @Override /* Overridden from InvocationHandler */
7102         public Object invoke(Object proxy, Method method, Object[] args) throws Throwable {
7103            var rim = rm.getMethodMeta(method);
7104
7105            var uri = rim.getUri();
7106            RestResponse res;
7107
7108            try {
7109               // @formatter:off
7110               RestRequest rc = request("POST", uri, true)
7111                  .serializer(serializer)
7112                  .content(args)
7113                  .rethrow(RuntimeException.class)
7114                  .rethrow(method.getExceptionTypes());
7115               // @formatter:on
7116
7117               res = rc.run();
7118
7119               var v = res.getContent().as(method.getGenericReturnType());
7120               if (v == null && method.getReturnType().isPrimitive())
7121                  v = ClassInfo.of(method.getReturnType()).getPrimitiveDefault();
7122               return v;
7123
7124            } catch (Exception e) {
7125               // Check if this is a RuntimeException wrapping a RestCallException with an Error cause
7126               if (e instanceof RuntimeException rex && rex.getCause() instanceof RestCallException rce) {
7127                  var t = rce.getCause();
7128                  if (nn(t)) {
7129                     // Check if the cause matches the method's declared exception types
7130                     for (var t2 : method.getExceptionTypes())
7131                        if (t2.isInstance(t))
7132                           throw t; // Rethrow the original Throwable (can be Error)
7133                  }
7134               }
7135               if (e instanceof RestCallException e2) {
7136                  var t = e2.getCause();
7137                  if (nn(t)) {
7138                     // Check if the cause matches the method's declared exception types
7139                     for (var t2 : method.getExceptionTypes())
7140                        if (t2.isInstance(t))
7141                           throw t; // Rethrow the original Throwable (can be Error)
7142                     if (t instanceof Exception ex)
7143                        e = ex;
7144                  }
7145               }
7146               if (e instanceof RuntimeException e2)
7147                  throw e2;
7148               for (var t2 : method.getExceptionTypes())
7149                  if (t2.isInstance(e))
7150                     throw e;
7151               throw toRex(e);
7152            }
7153         }
7154      });
7155   }

7235    *       <li>{@link URL}

7238    *    </ul>
7239    * @param body
7240    *    The object to serialize and transmit to the URI as the body of the request.
7241    *    Can be of the following types:
7242    *    <ul class='spaced-list'>
7243    *       <li>
7244    *          {@link Reader} - Raw contents of {@code Reader} will be serialized to remote resource.
7245    *       <li>
7246    *          {@link InputStream} - Raw contents of {@code InputStream} will be serialized to remote resource.
7247    *       <li>
7248    *          {@link HttpResource} - Raw contents will be serialized to remote resource.  Additional headers and media type will be set on request.
7249    *       <li>

7251    *       <li>

7253    *          {@link RestClient}.

7256    *       <li>
7257    *          {@link Supplier} - A supplier of anything on this list.
7258    *    </ul>
7259    * @return
7260    *    A {@link RestRequest} object that can be further tailored before executing the request and getting the response
7261    *    as a parsed object.

7513    * <p>
7514    * Typically you're going to use {@link #request(String, Object)} or {@link #request(String, Object, Object)},
7515    * but this method is provided to allow you to perform non-standard HTTP methods (e.g. HTTP FOO).
7516    *
7517    * @param method The method name (e.g. <js>"GET"</js>, <js>"OPTIONS"</js>).
7518    *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
7519    * @param uri
7520    *    The URI of the remote REST resource.
7521    *    <br>Can be any of the following types:
7522    *    <ul>
7523    *       <li>{@link URIBuilder}
7524    *       <li>{@link URI}
7525    *       <li>{@link URL}
7526    *       <li>{@link String}
7527    *       <li>{@link Object} - Converted to <c>String</c> using <c>toString()</c>
7528    *    </ul>
7529    *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (will result in an invalid request).
7530    * @param hasBody Boolean flag indicating if the specified request has content associated with it.
7531    * @return
7532    *    A {@link RestRequest} object that can be further tailored before executing the request and getting the response
7533    *    as a parsed object.
7534    * @throws RestCallException If any authentication errors occurred.
7535    */
7536   public RestRequest request(String method, Object uri, boolean hasBody) throws RestCallException {
7537      return request(op(method, uri, NO_BODY).hasContent(hasBody));
7538   }

7564    *       <li>

7764      } else if (logToConsole) {

7767            t.printStackTrace(console);
7768      }

7860   /**
7861    * Perform an arbitrary request against the specified URI.
7862    *
7863    * <p>
7864    * All requests feed through this method so it can be used to intercept request creations and make modifications
7865    * (such as add headers).
7866    *
7867    * @param op The operation that identifies the HTTP method, URL, and optional payload.
7868    * @return
7869    *    A {@link RestRequest} object that can be further tailored before executing the request and getting the response
7870    *    as a parsed object.
7871    * @throws RestCallException If any authentication errors occurred.
7872    */
7873   protected RestRequest request(RestOperation op) throws RestCallException {
7874      if (isClosed.get()) {
7875         Exception e2 = null;
7876         if (nn(closedStack)) {
7877            e2 = new Exception("Creation stack:");
7878            e2.setStackTrace(closedStack);
7879            throw new RestCallException(null, e2, "RestClient.close() has already been called.  This client cannot be reused.");
7880         }
7881         throw new RestCallException(null, null,
7882            "RestClient.close() has already been called.  This client cannot be reused.  Closed location stack trace can be displayed by setting the system property 'org.apache.juneau.rest.client2.RestClient.trackCreation' to true.");
7883      }
7884
7885      var req = createRequest(toUri(op.getUri(), rootUrl != null ? rootUrl.get() : null), op.getMethod(), op.hasContent());
7886
7887      onCallInit(req);
7888
7889      req.content(op.getContent());
7890
7891      return req;
7892   }

7909    *       target or by inspecting the request.
7910    * @param request The request to execute.
7911    * @param context The context to use for the execution, or <jk>null</jk> to use the default context.
7912    * @return
7913    *    The response to the request.
7914    *    <br>This is always a final response, never an intermediate response with an 1xx status code.
7915    *    <br>Whether redirects or authentication challenges will be returned or handled automatically depends on the
7916    *       implementation and configuration of this client.
7917    * @throws IOException In case of a problem or the connection was aborted.
7918    * @throws ClientProtocolException In case of an http protocol error.

7920   protected HttpResponse run(HttpHost target, HttpRequest request, HttpContext context) throws IOException {
7921      return callHandler.run(target, request, context);

7924   @SuppressWarnings({
7925      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for remote execution logic
7926      "java:S112",  // throws Exception intentional - callback/lifecycle method
7927      "unused"      // interfaceClass unused in this implementation but part of the remote execution contract
7928   })
7929   Object executeRemote(Class<?> interfaceClass, RestRequest rc, Method method, RemoteOperationMeta rom) throws Exception {
7930      RemoteOperationReturn ror = rom.getReturns();
7931
7932      try {
7933         Object ret = null;
7934            RestResponse res;
7935         rc.rethrow(RuntimeException.class);
7936         rom.forEachException(rc::rethrow);
7937         if (ror.getReturnValue() == RemoteReturn.NONE) {
7938            rc.complete();
7939         } else if (ror.getReturnValue() == RemoteReturn.STATUS) {
7940            res = rc.complete();
7941            int returnCode = res.getStatusCode();
7942            var rt = method.getReturnType();
7943            if (rt == Integer.class || rt == int.class)
7944               ret = returnCode;
7945            else if (rt == Boolean.class || rt == boolean.class)
7946               ret = returnCode < 400;
7947            else
7948               throw new RestCallException(res, null, "Invalid return type on method annotated with @RemoteOp(returns=RemoteReturn.STATUS).  Only integer and booleans types are valid.");
7949         } else if (ror.getReturnValue() == RemoteReturn.BEAN) {
7950            rc.ignoreErrors();
7951            res = rc.run();
7952            ret = res.as(ror.getResponseBeanMeta());
7953         } else if (ror.getReturnValue() == RemoteReturn.RESPONSE) {
7954            res = rc.run();
7955            ret = res;
7956         } else {
7957            var rt = method.getReturnType();
7958            if (Throwable.class.isAssignableFrom(rt))
7959               rc.ignoreErrors();
7960            res = rc.run();
7961            Object v = res.getContent().as(ror.getReturnType());
7962            if (v == null && rt.isPrimitive())
7963               v = ClassInfo.of(rt).getPrimitiveDefault();
7964            ret = v;
7965         }
7966         return ret;
7967      } catch (RestCallException e) {
7968         Throwable t = e.getCause();
7969         if (t instanceof RuntimeException t2)
7970            throw t2;
7971         if (t instanceof Exception ex) {
7972            for (var t3 : method.getExceptionTypes())
7973               if (t3.isInstance(ex))
7974                  throw ex;
7975         }
7976         // If cause is an Error that matches method's exception types, preserve it in RestCallException
7977         // The InvocationHandler will unwrap it and rethrow the original Error
7978         throw toRex(e);
7979      }
7980   }

7982   ExecutorService getExecutorService() {
7983      ExecutorService result = executorService.get();
7984      if (nn(result))
7985         return result;
7986      synchronized (this) {
7987         result = executorService.get();
7988         if (nn(result))
7989            return result;
7990         result = new ThreadPoolExecutor(1, 1, 30, TimeUnit.SECONDS, new ArrayBlockingQueue<>(10));
7991         executorService.set(result);
7992         return result;
7993      }
7994   }

7996   @SuppressWarnings({
7997      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires unchecked casts
7998   })
7999   <T extends Context> T getInstance(Class<T> c) {
8000      var o = requestContexts.computeIfAbsent(c, k -> {
8001         if (Serializer.class.isAssignableFrom(k)) {
8002            return Serializer.createSerializerBuilder((Class<? extends Serializer>)k).marshallingContext(getMarshallingContext()).build();
8003         } else if (Parser.class.isAssignableFrom(k)) {
8004            return Parser.createParserBuilder((Class<? extends Parser>)k).marshallingContext(getMarshallingContext()).build();
8005         }
8006         return null;
8007      });
8008      return (T)o;
8009   }

8011   /*
8012    * Returns the parser that best matches the specified content type.
8013    * If no match found or the content type is null, returns the parser in the list if it's a list of one.
8014    * Returns null if no parsers are defined.
8015    */
8016   Parser getMatchingParser(String mediaType) {
8017      if (parsers.isEmpty())
8018         return null;
8019      if (nn(mediaType)) {
8020         var p = parsers.getParser(mediaType).orElse(null);
8021         if (nn(p))
8022            return p;
8023      }
8024      var l = parsers.getParsers();
8025      return (l.size() == 1 ? l.get(0) : null);
8026   }
8027
8028   /*
8029    * Returns the serializer that best matches the specified content type.
8030    * If no match found or the content type is null, returns the serializer in the list if it's a list of one.
8031    * Returns null if no serializers are defined.
8032    */
8033   Serializer getMatchingSerializer(String mediaType) {
8034      if (serializers.isEmpty())
8035         return null;
8036      if (nn(mediaType)) {
8037         var s = serializers.getSerializer(mediaType).orElse(null);
8038         if (nn(s))
8039            return s;
8040      }
8041      var l = serializers.getSerializers();
8042      return (l.size() == 1 ? l.get(0) : null);
8043   }

8045   boolean hasParsers() {

8051   }

8053   URI toUri(Object x, String rootUrl) throws RestCallException {
8054      try {
8055         if (x instanceof URI x2)
8056            return x2;
8057         if (x instanceof URL x2)
8058            return x2.toURI();
8059         if (x instanceof URIBuilder x2)
8060            return x2.build();
8061         var s = x == null ? "" : x.toString();
8062         if (nn(rootUrl) && ! absUrlPattern.matcher(s).matches()) {
8063            if (s.isEmpty())
8064               s = rootUrl;
8065            else {
8066               var sb = new StringBuilder(rootUrl);
8067               if (! s.startsWith("/"))
8068                  sb.append('/');
8069               sb.append(s);
8070               s = sb.toString();
8071            }
8072         }
8073         s = fixUrl(s);
8074         return new URI(s);
8075      } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
8076         throw new RestCallException(null, e, "Invalid URI encountered:  {0}", x);  // Shouldn't happen.
8077      }
8078   }
8079}
